papers.title: Neuropharmacological Studies on Ibogaine, an Indole Alkaloid with Central-Stimulant Properties
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine produces EEG arousal via ascending reticular formation; effect blocked by atropine implicating cholinergic mechanism; paradoxical anticonvulsant properties despite central stimulation
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Psychotherapeutic Possibilities of New Fantasy-Enhancing Drugs
papers.key_findings: FOUNDATIONAL: First systematic phenomenological description of ibogaine/harmaline as 'oneirophrenics' - dream-like fantasy enhancement without psychosis. Coined term for ibogaine's unique mental state distinct from psychotomimetics.

papers.title: A Comparative Study on the Toxicity of Ibogaine and Serotonin
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine LD₅₀ 145 mg/kg rat i.p.; peaks liver/kidney 1h, cleared 12h; 15% excreted as indolic metabolite (not 5-HIAA); vagal-mediated hypotension/bradycardia (atropine-reversible); ethanol 2g/kg potentiates toxicity 1.4×
papers.dosing_range: Acute LD₅₀: 145 mg/kg i.p. (rat), 175 mg/kg i.p. (mouse), 327 mg/kg i.g. (rat). Chronic: 10–50 mg/kg i.p. (30 days), 40 mg/kg i.p. (12 days). Cardiovascular: 2–32 mg/kg i.v.

papers.title: Ibogaine and the inhibition of morphine self-administration: aftereffects of ibogaine on morphine self-administration in rats
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (10-80 mg/kg i.p.) dose-dependently reduced morphine self-administration in rats acutely and ≥1 day post-dose; some rats showed persistent decreases lasting weeks. Aftereffect not attributable to conditioned aversion or motor impairment.

papers.title: Interactions between ibogaine, a potential anti-addictive agent, and morphine: an in vivo microdialysis study
papers.key_findings: First in vivo microdialysis of ibogaine–morphine DA interactions. Ibogaine decreased striatal DA, increased prefrontal DA, and blocked morphine-induced DA rise 19 h post-dose across three brain regions.

papers.title: Mechanisms of action of ibogaine and harmaline congeners based on radioligand binding studies
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine binds κ-opioid receptors (Ki=2.08 μM) but not μ or δ via [³H]carfentanil; binding is reversible. Ibogaine and harmaline congeners interact with voltage-dependent sodium channels (μM range). No GABA interaction found.

papers.title: Effects of ibogaine on naloxone-precipitated withdrawal in morphine-dependent mice
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (30 mg/kg IP) failed to attenuate morphine withdrawal in mice and significantly potentiated naloxone-precipitated jumping (~9-fold), suggesting amplification of opioid-mediated responses rather than anti-withdrawal activity.

papers.title: Effects of Ibogaine on Acute Signs of Morphine Withdrawal in Rats: Independence from Tremor
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (40-80 mg/kg IP) significantly reduced 4 of 7 morphine withdrawal signs in rats; effects persisted 4 hr post-dose when tremors had fully dissipated, confirming independence from motor confounds.
papers.dosing_range: 20, 40 and 80 mg/kg IP (ibogaine HCl in saline, 1–4 ml/kg)

papers.title: Ibogaine Antagonizes Cocaine-Induced Locomotor Stimulation in Mice
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (40 mg/kg) reduces cocaine-induced locomotor stimulation in mice for ≥9 days. Selective for cocaine (not amphetamine). Transiently increases DA turnover (DOPAC+HVA/DA). Weak DAT affinity (IC₅₀ 1500 nM vs cocaine 120 nM).

papers.title: Inhibitory effects of ibogaine on cocaine self-administration in rats
papers.key_findings: Single ibogaine dose (40 mg/kg i.p.) reduced rat cocaine self-administration for >48h; weekly repeated dosing produced cumulative, more sustained inhibition suggesting long-lasting active metabolite(s)

papers.title: Pharmacodynamics and Therapeutic Applications of Iboga and Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive French-authored history of ibogaine research (1864–1993) documenting Lambert's early pharmacology, Lambarène product, Bwiti initiation ethnography, Naranjo's psychotherapy, and Lotsof's anti-addiction work
aliases: Pharmacodynamics and Therapeutic Applications of Iboga

papers.title: Degeneration of Purkinje Cells in Parasagittal Zones of the Cerebellar Vermis after Treatment with Ibogaine or Harmaline
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (100 mg/kg) and harmaline cause selective Purkinje cell degeneration in parasagittal zones of rat cerebellar vermis via proposed olivocerebellar excitotoxic mechanism. Doses 4× proposed human dose.

papers.title: Effects of iboga alkaloids on morphine and cocaine self-administration in rats: relationship to tremorigenic effects and to effects on dopamine release in nucleus accumbens and striatum
papers.key_findings: R-enantiomers of ibogamine/coronaridine reduced morphine & cocaine self-admin without tremor; R-isomers decreased DA in NAC/STR while S-isomers had no effect. Anti-addictive and tremorigenic effects dissociable.

papers.title: The putative anti-addictive drug ibogaine is a competitive inhibitor of [3H]MK-801 binding to the NMDA receptor complex
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine competitively inhibits [³H]MK-801 binding at NMDA receptor ion channels (Kᵢ = 1.01 ± 0.1 μM, nH = 1.2). No effect on AMPA, kainate, or metabotropic glutamate receptors up to 100 μM.
papers.publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC

papers.title: Ibogaine Reduces Preference for Cocaine Consumption in C57BL/6By Mice
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (2×40 mg/kg IP) reduced cocaine preference from 71% to 41% and intake by 38% for ≥5 days in C57BL/6By mice. Attenuated cocaine-induced locomotor sensitisation independently of brain cocaine levels.
papers.dosing_range: 2×40 mg/kg IP ibogaine HCl (6 h apart); cocaine 200–400 mg/l oral, 25 mg/kg SC challenge

papers.title: A Preliminary Investigation of Ibogaine: Case Reports and Recommendations for Further Study
papers.key_findings: First published case series (N=7): ibogaine 700–1800 mg (11.7–25 mg/kg) abolished opiate withdrawal in all subjects; 3/7 remained drug-free ≥14 weeks; higher doses correlated with better outcomes.

papers.title: Ibogaine and Its Congeners Are σ Receptor-Selective Ligands with Moderate Affinity
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine binds σ₂ receptors (Kᵢ = 201 nM) with 43-fold selectivity over σ₁ — highest affinity target known at time. O-desmethylibogaine loses σ₂ affinity (Kᵢ = 5226 nM), suggesting demethylation terminates σ-mediated effects.

papers.title: Effects of ibogaine on responding maintained by food, cocaine and heroin reinforcement in rats
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (80 mg/kg IP) suppressed food, cocaine, and heroin self-administration in rats; heroin responding most sensitive. Effects not drug-specific — motor impairment may contribute.
papers.dosing_range: 40 and 80 mg/kg IP (ibogaine in water, 10 mg/ml)
aliases: Ibogaine Food Cocaine Heroin Self-Administration Rats
papers.publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC

papers.title: Identification and Quantitation of Ibogaine and an o-Demethylated Metabolite in Brain and Biological Fluids Using Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry
papers.key_findings: First validated GC-MS method for ibogaine and noribogaine (12-OH-ibogamine) in biological fluids/brain. LOD 5ng/mL. Identified noribogaine as major metabolite. Foundation for pharmacokinetic research.

papers.title: Identification of a Primary Metabolite of Ibogaine That Targets Serotonin Transporters and Elevates Serotonin
papers.key_findings: First identification of noribogaine (12-hydroxyibogamine) as ibogaine's primary metabolite; Kᵢ 40.7 nM at SERT (10-fold > ibogaine); 3-fold 5-HT elevation in nucleus accumbens; long half-life with 508–706 ng/ml at 24h post-dose

papers.title: Radioligand-binding study of noribogaine, a likely metabolite of ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine shows 3-4× higher affinity than ibogaine at κ (Kᵢ=0.96 μM vs 3.77 μM) and μ (Kᵢ=2.66 μM vs 11.04 μM) opioid receptors, and unlike ibogaine binds δ receptors (Kᵢ=24.72 μM)

papers.title: 100 Years of Ibogaine: Neurochemical and Pharmacological Actions of a Putative Anti-addictive Drug
papers.key_findings: LANDMARK REVIEW: Comprehensive 100-year overview of ibogaine pharmacology from NIH team. Covers NMDA antagonism, sigma receptor binding, dopamine modulation. Essential reference for mechanism understanding.

papers.title: Attenuation of Alcohol Intake by Ibogaine in Three Strains of Alcohol-Preferring Rats
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine IP/IG (not SC) dose-dependently reduced alcohol intake in P, FH, AA rats; 60 mg/kg IP: 51–65% reduction; 5-day IG dosing showed no tolerance; no effect on blood alcohol; suggests hepatic metabolic activation required

papers.title: Receptor binding profile suggests multiple mechanisms of action are responsible for ibogaine's putative anti-addictive activity
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine binds 14 receptors at 1–100 μM: dopamine uptake IC₅₀ 3.5 μM, NMDA/MK-801 5.6 μM, 5-HT₃ 3.9 μM, 5-HT₂ 4.8 μM, κ-opioid 16 μM; no nanomolar affinity at any site.

papers.title: Neurochemical and Behavioural Interactions Between Ibogaine and Nicotine in the Rat
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (40 mg/kg IP) attenuated nicotine-induced NAc dopamine overflow 22h post-treatment but not locomotion; produced long-lasting anxiogenesis and mPFC neurochemical changes persisting ≥7 days
papers.dosing_range: 40 mg/kg IP (ibogaine in 25% ethanol/sterile water, 1.0 ml/kg)

papers.title: Ibogaine Block of the NMDA Receptor: In Vitro and In Vivo Studies
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine is an open channel NMDA receptor antagonist (IC₅₀ 3.1 µM, -60 mV). Block is use- and voltage-dependent, occluded by Mg²⁺. In vivo MES protection ED₅₀ 31 mg/kg i.p.; LD₅₀ 213 mg/kg in mice.

papers.title: 18-Methoxycoronaridine, a non-toxic iboga alkaloid congener: effects on morphine and cocaine self-administration and on mesolimbic dopamine release in rats
papers.key_findings: BREAKTHROUGH: First demonstration that 18-MC provides equivalent anti-addictive efficacy to ibogaine (decreases morphine/cocaine self-admin) without tremors or cerebellar toxicity. Safer ibogaine analogue.

papers.title: Tissue Distribution of Ibogaine After Intraperitoneal and Subcutaneous Administration
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine accumulates 100-fold in fat vs plasma after 40 mg/kg in rats; sc route yields higher levels than ip (first-pass effect); adipose reservoir may explain prolonged duration of action

papers.title: Structurally modified ibogaine analogs exhibit differing affinities for NMDA receptors
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine most potent NMDA antagonist in series (Kᵢ = 1.1 μM); O-desmethylibogaine ~5× weaker. Only ibogaine attenuated naloxone-precipitated withdrawal in mice, supporting NMDA-mediated anti-addictive mechanism.

papers.title: Ibogaine, Trauma, and Abreaction: The Treatment of Chemical Dependence
papers.key_findings: FOUNDATIONAL CLINICAL: First documentation of ibogaine-induced abreaction and trauma processing. Ibogaine eliminates withdrawal while enabling non-emotional trauma access - key psychological mechanism for addiction treatment.
papers.dosing_range: 10 mg/kg (PK study) and 20 mg/kg (therapeutic) oral — Case 2 only; doses not specified for Cases 1 and 3

papers.title: Ibogaine neurotoxicity: a re-evaluation
papers.key_findings: 40 mg/kg ibogaine (anti-addictive dose) produced no cerebellar Purkinje cell degeneration in rats; only high doses (≥100 mg/kg) caused degeneration, confined to lobules 5-7 and simplex. Anti-addictive and neurotoxic properties are dissociable.

papers.title: Acute and Chronic Administration of Ibogaine to the Rat Results in Astrogliosis That Is Not Confined to the Cerebellar Vermis
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine causes dose-, sex-, region-dependent astrogliosis in rats. Chronic oral dosing: up to 215% GFAP increases in female hippocampus, brain stem, striatum; males unaffected. NF-68/beta-tubulin >2-fold in females. Gliosis beyond cerebellar vermis.
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Neuropharmacological characterization of local ibogaine effects on dopamine release
papers.key_findings: BIPHASIC MECHANISM: Local ibogaine shows dose-dependent dual effects - low doses (10⁻⁶-10⁻⁴M) decrease dopamine via kappa receptors, high doses (10⁻³M) increase dopamine via DAT interaction.
papers.publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC

papers.title: Ibogaine Produces Neurodegeneration in Rat, but Not Mouse, Cerebellum: Neurohistological Biomarkers of Purkinje Cell Loss
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine 100 mg/kg i.p. causes parasagittal Purkinje cell degeneration in rat cerebellum (silver stain, calbindin loss, GFAP astrocytosis) but no neurotoxicity in mice at same dose. H&E staining insufficient—risks false negatives.
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Pharmacological screen for activities of 12-hydroxyibogamine: a primary metabolite of the indole alkaloid ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine 10× more potent than ibogaine at SERT (IC₅₀ 40.7 nM vs 590 nM). Both ~equipotent at DAT (~3-4 μM). Brain levels 1-17 μM post-oral dosing; metabolite rises 10-fold over 2h as parent declines.

papers.title: Time-dependent interactions between iboga agents and cocaine
papers.key_findings: BIPHASIC TIMING: All iboga agents (ibogaine, noribogaine, 18-MC) inhibit cocaine hyperactivity at 1h but potentiate it at 19h post-administration. Explains divergent literature findings.

papers.title: 18-Methoxycoronaridine attenuates nicotine-induced dopamine release and nicotine preferences in rats
papers.key_findings: THERAPEUTIC EXPANSION: 18-MC extends to nicotine addiction - attenuates nicotine-induced NAc dopamine release (19h pretreatment) and abolishes nicotine preference 24h+. Reduces nicotine without affecting water intake.
papers.publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC

papers.title: Mechanisms of Antiaddictive Actions of Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine antiaddictive effects via κ-opioid + NMDA (opioids/stimulants), SERT (alcohol), nAChR antagonism (nicotine); σ₂ mediates neurotoxicity; 18-MC dissociates efficacy from toxicity.
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Observations on Treatment with Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine 20-25 mg/kg caused transient vestibulocerebellar dysfunction (nystagmus, intention tremor, ataxia) resolving by 24h; no ECG or EEG abnormalities; no withdrawal/craving at 24h
papers.dosing_range: 1 mg/kg test dose (day 1); 20–25 mg/kg ibogaine HCl full dose (day 2)

papers.title: Ibogaine acts at the nicotinic acetylcholine receptor cation channel to inhibit catecholamine release
papers.key_findings: MECHANISM: Low-dose ibogaine (10µM) selectively inhibits nicotinic receptor-mediated catecholamine release without affecting K+ or veratridine-evoked release. Not kappa-opioid mediated. Concentration-dependent reversibility.

papers.title: Medication Development of Ibogaine as a Pharmacotherapy for Drug Dependence
papers.key_findings: First human PK data: ibogaine peaks ~4 hr (600–1250 ng/ml), noribogaine persists >24 hr (~800 ng/ml). Identified noribogaine as active metabolite with μ-opioid partial agonist activity (Ki ~200 nM). 30 subjects, no significant AEs at 500–800 mg.
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Cytochrome P4502D6 Catalyzes the O-Demethylation of the Psychoactive Alkaloid Ibogaine to 12-Hydroxyibogamine
papers.key_findings: CYP2D6 is the primary enzyme catalysing ibogaine O-demethylation to noribogaine (KMapp 1.1 µM, IC₅₀ quinidine 0.2 µM, r²=0.711 with bufuralol 1'-hydroxylase). Poor metabolisers lack this activity.

papers.title: Noribogaine stimulates naloxone-sensitive [³⁵S]GTPγS binding
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine is a full μ-opioid agonist (EC₅₀ 324 nM, 170% maximal stimulation) comparable to DAMGO and morphine; ibogaine has negligible intrinsic activity. Identical G-protein population activated.

papers.title: Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: JOURNALISM: Comprehensive 1998 landscape review documenting Lotsof's 1962 discovery, treatment providers (Lotsof/Panama, Mash/St Kitts, Taub/Costa Rica), costs ($2,200-$20,000), and first-person experience reports.

papers.title: Treatment of Acute Opioid Withdrawal with Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: LANDMARK CASE SERIES: n=33 opioid-dependent patients (avg 0.64g heroin/day IV). 76% (25/33) achieved complete withdrawal resolution within 24h sustained 72h. One fatality (possible surreptitious heroin use).

papers.title: (±)-18-Methoxycoronaridine: A Novel Iboga Alkaloid Congener Having Potential Anti-Addictive Efficacy
papers.key_findings: DEFINITIVE 18-MC REVIEW: Synthesises preclinical evidence. Zero tremors/cerebellar toxicity at 100mg/kg (vs ibogaine Purkinje damage). Equivalent anti-addictive efficacy across morphine, cocaine, alcohol, nicotine.

papers.title: Attenuation of the reinforcing efficacy of morphine by 18-methoxycoronaridine
papers.key_findings: 18-MC (40 mg/kg, 19 h pre) inhibits morphine-induced NAc dopamine release while enhancing metabolites. Downward shift in morphine dose–response — reduces reinforcing efficacy without altering potency.

papers.title: Ibogaine in Acute Opioid Withdrawal: An Open Label Case Series
papers.key_findings: NYAS proceedings of Alper1999 case series: 33 opioid-dependent patients, 76% (25/33) complete withdrawal resolution within 24h sustained 72h. One fatality. Discusses 18-MC as potential lower-psychoactivity congener.
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Application of Electrophysiological Method to Study Interactions between Ibogaine and Cocaine
papers.key_findings: ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY: IBO pretreatment (50mg/kg, 1h prior) dramatically altered cocaine ECoG response - increased delta/theta/alpha1 power vs cocaine alone. DA levels decreased further with IBO/COC combination. Suggests decreased cocaine response.
papers.dosing_range: 50 mg/kg ibogaine (rat i.p.); 20 mg/kg cocaine (rat i.p.)
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: 18-Methoxycoronaridine (18-MC) and Ibogaine: Comparison of Antiaddictive Efficacy, Toxicity, and Mechanisms of Action
papers.key_findings: 18-MC vs ibogaine comparison: equal anti-addictive efficacy for morphine, cocaine, alcohol, nicotine. 18-MC lacks tremor, cerebellar damage, bradycardia. Lower NMDA/sigma-2 affinity. Greater therapeutic index than ibogaine.

papers.title: Development of Novel Medications for Drug Addiction: The Legacy of an African Shrub
papers.key_findings: 18-MC matches ibogaine anti-addictive efficacy across morphine, cocaine, alcohol, nicotine without tremor or bradycardia. Downward shift in dose-response suggests reduced reinforcing efficacy.
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Neurologic, Electroencephalographic and General Medical Observations in Subjects Administered Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: First human EEG study during ibogaine — all recordings normal. Universal transient cerebellar ataxia resolved by 24h. Movement-induced vomiting (3/5). No withdrawal symptoms at 24h; 2 subjects symptom-free at 1 week.
papers.dosing_range: 1 mg/kg test dose + 0.5 mg/kg sensitivity test + ~25 mg/kg treatment dose oral (ibogaine HCl); additional 5 mg/kg oral if early vomiting; rectal re-administration used in 1 subject with persistent vomiting

papers.title: Ibogaine: Complex Pharmacokinetics, Concerns for Safety, and Preliminary Efficacy Measures
papers.key_findings: Preliminary efficacy (n=27, 500–800 mg ibogaine HCl): BDI depression scores fell significantly (16.86→2.29, p=.0005). Opiate craving reduced across all 5 HCQN-29 subscales (p<.0001). Cocaine craving reduced on 3 of 5 CCQN-45 subscales.
papers.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Pharmacological comparison of the effect of ibogaine and 18-methoxycoronaridine on isolated smooth muscle from the rat and guinea-pig
papers.key_findings: PERIPHERAL: No evidence for µ-opioid receptor activity - naloxone-insensitive inhibition of ileum contractions. Novel finding: both agents enhance purinergic contractions 3-5 fold. Ibogaine enhances portal vein contractions; 18-MC does not.

papers.title: A Dose-Response Study of Ibogaine-Induced Neuropathology in the Rat Cerebellum
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine ip NOAEL 25 mg/kg in female rats. Purkinje cell degeneration dose-dependent: 0/6 at 25, 2/6 at 50, 6/6 at 75–100 mg/kg. GFAP most sensitive biomarker.

papers.title: A Contemporary History of Ibogaine in the United States and Europe
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive narrative of ibogaine treatment scenes in US and Europe 1962-2001, documenting Lotsof's discovery, advocacy movements, and regulatory landscape

papers.title: Ibogaine: A Review
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive ibogaine pharmacology synthesis: NMDA, opioid, 5-HT, nicotinic interactions. Novel mechanism distinct from existing treatments. 10 μM brain levels validate in vitro activities.

papers.title: In Vivo Neurobiological Effects of Ibogaine and Its O-Desmethyl Metabolite, 12-Hydroxyibogamine (Noribogaine), in Rats
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine is ~10× more potent than ibogaine as indirect 5-HT agonist via SERT inhibition. Ibogaine causes tremors/ataxia; noribogaine does not. Neither drug alters accumbens DA. Noribogaine may be safer alternative.
papers.journal: Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics

papers.title: Returning to the Path: The Use of Iboga[ine] in an Equatorial African Ritual Context and the Binding of Time, Space, and Social Relationships
papers.key_findings: Reframes Bwiti iboga use as 'time and space binding' against colonial anomie; compares initiation with INTASH therapy; argues collectivist aftercare model outperforms Western individualism

papers.title: From the Roots Up: Ibogaine and Addict Self-Help
papers.key_findings: Dutch addict self-help movement (INTASH) treated 8 opiate-dependent subjects with ibogaine 20 mg/kg; 7/8 achieved 6-month abstinence with complete acute withdrawal resolution
papers.dosing_range: 20 mg/kg ibogaine HCl oral (capsules, single dose) for addiction interruption; 10 mg/kg for initiatory sessions

papers.title: Mechanisms of Action of Ibogaine: Relevance to Putative Therapeutic Effects and Development of a Safer Iboga Alkaloid Congener
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive receptor screen of ibogaine, noribogaine, and 18-MC across 23 binding sites; 18-MC mimics antiaddictive efficacy without tremor, cerebellar toxicity, or cardiac effects; both reverse sensitised dopamine responses.

papers.title: Case Studies of Ibogaine Treatment: Implications for Patient Management Strategies
papers.key_findings: Three case studies across drug user, self-help, and clinical settings document opioid withdrawal attenuation and craving reduction; richer oneirophrenic experiences correlate with longer drug-free periods.
papers.dosing_range: 0.14–19 mg/kg oral (1962 group); 100 mg test dose + 1200 mg therapeutic (ICASH, ~1989); 100 mg + 25 mg test doses, 10 mg/kg experimental, 20 mg/kg therapeutic (Panama clinical)

papers.title: Ibogaine in the Treatment of Heroin Withdrawal
papers.key_findings: PHARMACOKINETICS: Ibogaine peaks ~1h (3.2±1.3 µg/mL at 8-12mg/kg), T½ 7.3±3.2h. Noribogaine appears within 2h, persists 24-48+ hours. CYP2D6 polymorphisms affect metabolism. Establishes dose-PK relationships.

papers.title: Ibogaine: A Novel Anti-Addictive Compound - A Comprehensive Literature Review
papers.key_findings: ACADEMIC REVIEW: Comprehensive UMBC thesis covering Lotsof history 1962-2002, pharmacology (CYP2D6→noribogaine, multi-receptor profile), 3 fatalities (1989-2000), Purkinje toxicity >75mg/kg, 18-MC as non-toxic alternative.

papers.title: Ibogaine Related Fatality
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine fatality in 36yo male polydrug user; tissue concentrations: subclavian blood 9225 ng/mL, brain 18563 ng/kg, liver 18137 ng/kg — extremely high levels
contraindications: Concurrent substance abuse (morphine, cocaine metabolites detected)
Polydrug use with alcohol (empty bottles at scene)
papers.dosing_range: unknown (ibogaine source and dose not specified; blood level 9225 ng/mL suggests massive exposure, brain/liver concentrations 18137–18563 ng/kg)

papers.title: Enantioselective Synthesis of Key Intermediates in a Novel Approach towards the Iboga-Alkaloid Family
papers.key_findings: Improved enantioselective synthesis of isoquinuclidine intermediates for iboga alkaloids from L-glutamic acid via intramolecular 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition; both antipodal series accessible.

papers.title: Life after Ibogaine: An exploratory study of the long-term effects of ibogaine treatment on drug addicts
papers.key_findings: VU AMSTERDAM STUDY: n=21, 87% opioid primary. Drug-free periods: 1 treatment=9.7mo, 2 treatments=35.7mo, 3 treatments=52mo. HCl vs extract: 26.8 vs 2.1mo abstinence. 92% reported anxiety improvement.

papers.title: Combating Substance Abuse with Ibogaine: Pre- and Posttreatment Recommendations and an Example of Successive Model Fitting Analyses
papers.key_findings: Dose-to-weight ratio predicts heroin abstinence (sigmoidal R²=.88); threshold ~16 mg/kg; pre/posttreatment psychosocial recommendations proposed
contraindications: Active psychosis or psychosis-prone individuals (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder)
papers.dosing_range: 700–1800 mg oral ibogaine HCl (11.7–25.0 mg/kg DWR); test dose 100–200 mg; threshold ~16 mg/kg

papers.title: Glial Cell Line-Derived Neurotrophic Factor Mediates the Desirable Actions of the Anti-addiction Drug Ibogaine against Alcohol Consumption
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine reduces ethanol self-admin via VTA GDNF upregulation. Intra-VTA GDNF mimics effect; anti-GDNF antibodies attenuate it (79% vs 39% reduction). No neurotoxicity at 40 mg/kg.

papers.title: Ibogaine in the 21st Century: Boosters, Tune-ups and Maintenance
papers.key_findings: Landmark MAPS Bulletin introducing booster/maintenance protocols: 500-800mg follow-up doses during noribogaine window extend benefits, reduce relapse. Clean maintenance (drug-free) vs dirty maintenance (harm reduction) protocols.
contraindications: Concurrent opiate/opioid use at higher ibogaine doses — ibogaine potentiates analgesic effect, difference between 50 mg and 500 mg described as 'quite possibly fatal'
Maintenance regimen beyond 20-25 days without break — jitteriness, sleep disturbance, overstimulation
papers.dosing_range: Flood 16–18 mg/kg; boosters/tune-ups 500–800 mg; clean maintenance 50 mg 2x/week + 100 mg every 45 days; dirty maintenance 25–50 mg/day oral

papers.title: Ibogaine Therapy: A 'Vast, Uncontrolled Experiment'
papers.key_findings: News feature documenting >5000 ibogaine treatments worldwide by 2005, 30-40 clinics operating, upcoming Mash phase I trial and Israeli efficacy study, Ron GDNF findings, and Glick 18-MC development.
contraindications: Irregular cardiac rhythms (exclusion criterion at St. Kitts clinic)

papers.title: Autoregulation of glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor expression: implications for the long-lasting actions of the anti-addiction drug, Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: FOUNDATIONAL: First demonstration that ibogaine upregulates GDNF via autoregulatory feedback loop. 10µM ibogaine induces sustained GDNF mRNA increase (48h+). GDNF positively regulates own expression - mechanism for long-term anti-addiction effects.
papers.dosing_range: 10 µM ibogaine HCl in cell culture

papers.title: Simultaneous quantification of ibogaine and noribogaine in human plasma and whole blood by liquid chromatography with electrospray ionization mass spectrometric detection
papers.key_findings: First validated LC-ESI-MS method for ibogaine/noribogaine in plasma (LLOQ 0.89/1 µg/L) and whole blood (LLOQ 1.78/2 µg/kg). Fatal poisoning case: blood levels 10.8 µg/g ibogaine, 20.8 µg/g noribogaine (10–20× therapeutic).

papers.title: Distribution of Ibogaine and Noribogaine in a Man Following a Poisoning Involving Root Bark of the Tabernanthe iboga Shrub
papers.key_findings: First human tissue distribution of ibogaine/noribogaine post-mortem. Highest concentrations in lung (50.1/55.9 µg/g), liver (40.5/50.5 µg/g), brain (12.5/18.7 µg/g). Blood levels 5–20× therapeutic range.
papers.dosing_range: unknown (root bark, ~18 spoonfuls over ~10 hours, blood levels 5–20× therapeutic)

papers.title: Fatalities after taking ibogaine in addiction treatment could be related to sudden cardiac death caused by autonomic dysfunction
papers.key_findings: Hypothesises ibogaine fatalities are sudden cardiac deaths from autonomic dysregulation; Bwiti ritual maintains right-hemispheric trance as cardioprotective mechanism
contraindications: Psychiatric conditions with autonomic dysregulation (schizophrenia, PTSD, depression)
papers.dosing_range: variable — fatalities after doses from sub-therapeutic (<10 mg/kg) to standard therapeutic (20–30 mg/kg); exact doses often unknown in retrospective cases

papers.title: Ibogaine affects brain energy metabolism
papers.key_findings: NOVEL MECHANISM: Ibogaine induces glycolysis/TCA cycle enzymes (GAPDH, aldolase A, pyruvate kinase, malate dehydrogenase) at 24-72h. Anti-addiction effect may be mediated by enhanced energy availability for neuroplasticity.

papers.title: The Use of Ibogaine in the Treatment of Addictions
papers.key_findings: DEFINITIVE CLINICAL OVERVIEW: Comprehensive Alper/Lotsof chapter covering mechanisms, dosing protocols, safety considerations, global treatment landscape, and NIDA research history. Key reference for ibogaine clinical practice.

papers.title: The ibogaine medical subculture
papers.key_findings: 3414 individuals took ibogaine by Feb 2006 (4× increase over 5yr); 68% for substance disorders, 53% for opioid withdrawal; 11 fatalities reported 1990–2006, mostly cardiac-related with pre-existing risk factors
contraindications: Cardiac disease (prior MI, cardiomyopathy, valvular disease)

papers.title: Determination of ibogaine and noribogaine in biological fluids and hair by LC–MS/MS after Tabernanthe iboga abuse: Iboga alkaloids distribution in a drowning death case
papers.key_findings: Post-mortem ibogaine/noribogaine distribution in 9 tissue types after iboga-related drowning; first report of ibogaine detection in hair (1.2/2.5 ng/mg)
contraindications: Use in uncontrolled settings (Bwiti ceremony context)
papers.dosing_range: unknown (iboga root bark; blood levels elevated relative to published therapeutic range — femoral ibogaine 3.3 µg/mL, noribogaine 4.6 µg/mL)

papers.title: Ibogaine, an anti-addictive drug: pharmacology and time to go further in development. A narrative review
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive pharmacology review: noribogaine 10× more potent at SERT (IC₅₀ 0.04 vs 0.59 μM), full μ-opioid agonist (IC₅₀ 0.16 vs 11.0 μM); CYP2D6 polymorphism yields 9× lower noribogaine Cmax in PMs
contraindications: CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (altered ibogaine/noribogaine ratio)
Pre-existing cardiac conditions (dose-dependent cardiotoxicity in preclinical models)

papers.title: Long-QT Syndrome Induced by the Antiaddiction Drug Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: First published case of ibogaine-induced Long-QT syndrome (QTc 616 ms) with ventricular tachyarrhythmias in a 31-year-old woman; QT normalised at 42 hours; concurrent hypomagnesaemia and hypokalaemia identified.

papers.title: Iboga: Between Myth and Reality
papers.key_findings: COMPREHENSIVE REVIEW: French-language synthesis of iboga's dual identity - traditional Bwiti medicine (convalescence, fatigue, spiritual ritual) and modern anti-addiction research. Bridges cultural and scientific perspectives.

papers.title: Interaction of ibogaine with human α3β4-nicotinic acetylcholine receptors in different conformational states
papers.key_findings: RECEPTOR MECHANISM: Ibogaine inhibits human α3β4 nAChRs with state-dependent binding - higher affinity for desensitised states. Noncompetitive mechanism supports nicotinic modulation as key anti-addiction pathway.
papers.journal: International Journal of Biochemistry & Cell Biology

papers.title: Noribogaine, but not 18-MC, exhibits similar actions as ibogaine on GDNF expression and ethanol self-administration
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine increases GDNF expression like ibogaine; 18-MC does not. Intra-VTA noribogaine reduces alcohol self-administration; 18-MC does not. Different mechanisms despite similar anti-addictive profiles. Key for analogue development.

papers.title: Induction of energy metabolism related enzymes in yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae exposed to ibogaine is adaptation to acute decrease in ATP energy pool
papers.key_findings: MECHANISM CONFIRMATION: Yeast model shows ibogaine causes acute ATP decrease triggering compensatory enzyme induction. Supports energy metabolism hypothesis - neuroplasticity may require enhanced metabolic capacity.

papers.title: Psychedelics and the Human Receptorome
papers.key_findings: NIMH-PDSP receptorome study: 35 psychedelics at 51 receptor sites. Table S1 comprehensive ibogaine/noribogaine Ki data: high σ2, κ-opioid, SERT affinity. Novel Ki normalization method for comparing multi-receptor profiles.

papers.title: Ibogaine and the inhibition of acetylcholinesterase
papers.key_findings: NOVEL MECHANISM: Ibogaine inhibits acetylcholinesterase, contributing to muscarinic cholinergic effects. Adds to multi-receptor profile and may explain some pharmacological/toxicological properties.

papers.title: The Need for Ibogaine in Drug and Alcohol Addiction Treatment
papers.key_findings: Legal commentary argues ibogaine should be rescheduled from Schedule I to II in the US, citing 83% withdrawal-free outcomes, societal costs of $181B/yr for drug abuse, and low abuse potential
contraindications: ECG abnormalities or previous heart problems
Severe mental health problems (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder)
HIV positive or Hepatitis C symptomatic
Long-term medication with no prior data on ibogaine interactions
Narcotic analgesics, cocaine, amphetamines, or alcohol use within 12 hours prior to treatment
aliases: Ibogaine Drug Alcohol Addiction Legal Commentary
Need for Ibogaine in Addiction Treatment

papers.title: Ibogaine-Associated Psychosis in Schizophrenia: A Case Report
papers.key_findings: First report of ibogaine-associated psychosis in schizophrenia; recommends excluding schizophrenia from ibogaine trials and screening for ibogaine use in shamanic-ritual psychosis
contraindications: Attenuated psychotic symptoms or prodromal psychosis
papers.dosing_range: unquantified (Iboga powder, small amounts escalating to daily use over 2 months)

papers.title: The Visionary Cure of the Addiction War? Ibogaine: Social Context, Subcultural Identity, and Implications for Drug Policy
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine treatment attracts addicts through subcultural identity formation that inverts dominant culture values; 3,414 treatments by 2006, 12 deaths since 1990

papers.title: Fatalities Temporally Associated with the Ingestion of Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: FOUNDATIONAL: Systematic review of all 19 ibogaine fatalities (1990-2008). 12/14 adequate autopsies showed cardiovascular comorbidities + polysubstance intoxication explained deaths. No characteristic neurotoxicity syndrome.

papers.title: Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Maryland Poison Center alert: ibogaine toxicity includes QTc prolongation, bradycardia, VT, TdP, respiratory arrest; cardiac effects may persist up to 9 days; no specific antidote
contraindications: Concomitant use of opioids (active use or inadequate taper)
Concomitant use of benzodiazepines or cocaine
Seizure risk from alcohol or benzodiazepine withdrawal
Use of ibogaine extract or root bark instead of ibogaine HCl
Administration without close medical observation and monitoring
papers.dosing_range: not specified (administered as ibogaine HCl 95–98% pure, ibogaine extracts, or dried root bark containing 15–50% iboga alkaloids)
aliases: Maryland Poison Center Ibogaine Toxicity Alert

papers.title: Anti-addiction drug ibogaine inhibits hERG channels: a cardiac arrhythmia risk
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine blocks hERG channels with IC₅₀ of 4 μM, overlapping therapeutic plasma concentrations (low μM range), providing first mechanistic evidence for ibogaine-induced QT prolongation and cardiac arrhythmia risk.
papers.dosing_range: IC₅₀ 3.9 μM — in vitro hERG patch-clamp; overlaps therapeutic plasma range (low μM)
aliases: Ibogaine hERG Channel Blockade Brief Communication

papers.title: Life-threatening complications of ibogaine: three case reports
papers.key_findings: Three Dutch case reports: QTc >700ms with torsade de pointes, respiratory depression requiring ventilation. Naranjo scale confirms probable ibogaine causality. Authors warn of unregulated online availability.

papers.title: Metabolic plasticity and the energy economizing effect of ibogaine, the principal alkaloid of Tabernanthe iboga
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine triggers dose-dependent biphasic CO₂ elevation in yeast (peak +142% at 20 mg/l), reduces oxidative load 24–57% via pro-antioxidant mechanism, and establishes lower-energy metabolic equilibrium.

papers.title: Ibogaine-associated ventricular tachyarrhythmias
papers.key_findings: Single 600mg ibogaine dose caused 5 VFs and 3 VTs over 48h with QTc prolongation to 593ms at 42h. Amiodarone worsened QTc. Vagal maneuvers triggered arrhythmias. QTc normalised day 9.

papers.title: Pause-dependent ventricular tachycardia and torsades de pointes after ibogaine ingestion
papers.key_findings: First documented case of pause-dependent VT and TdP after ibogaine in a patient with normal pretreatment screening, demonstrating screening limitations.
contraindications: Concurrent or recent methadone use (patient transitioned off one month prior)
Aggressive cathartic use prior to ibogaine (caused persistent hypokalemia)
Combined ibogaine HCl and root bark extract administration (dual-source dosing)
Non-medical setting without continuous cardiac monitoring
papers.dosing_range: 10.5 mg/kg ibogaine HCl (85–98% purity) + 14 mg/kg T. iboga root bark extract (50% alkaloids) over 15 hours

papers.title: The Vanishing World of Iboga: And a Closer Look at Sustainable Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Conference presentation on T. iboga endangerment from over-harvesting. Documents black market crisis in Gabon/Cameroon. Discusses Voacanga africana as sustainable alternative source for ibogaine synthesis.

papers.title: Ibogaine for opioid addiction: A Deadly treatment
papers.key_findings: Fatal ibogaine case in 25-year-old with pre-existing cardiac dysrhythmias and AICD; postmortem ibogaine levels: heart blood 2.2 mcg/ml, liver 4.2 mcg/g.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac dysrhythmias with AICD placement
History of heroin abuse (substance use history)

papers.title: Effect of Iboga Alkaloids on µ-Opioid Receptor-Coupled G Protein Activation
papers.key_findings: CRITICAL MECHANISM FINDING: Ibogaine, noribogaine, 18-MC are MOR ANTAGONISTS (Ki 3-13µM), NOT agonists. Opioid withdrawal attenuation cannot be explained by MOR agonism - novel mechanism required.

papers.title: Growing Menace of Ibogaine Toxicity
papers.key_findings: First UK ibogaine toxicity case: 7g oral dose caused QTc 600ms, TdP cardiac arrest, seizures; rescued by defibrillation and transcutaneous overdrive pacing at 80 bpm for 48h.

papers.title: Ibogaine in the Treatment of Substance Dependence
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive narrative review covering ibogaine history, preclinical evidence, safety concerns (19 fatalities to 2008), and human efficacy data; identifies ibogaine medical subculture growth and need for controlled trials.

papers.title: Unique and potent effects of acute ibogaine on zebrafish: The developing utility of novel aquatic models for hallucinogenic drug research
papers.key_findings: Zebrafish ibogaine exposure (10–20 mg/L) reverses geotaxis and scototaxis, disrupts shoaling, evokes melanophore darkening, and validates aquatic 3D phenotyping for hallucinogen research.

papers.title: A Case of Death Due to Ibogaine Use for Heroin Addiction
papers.key_findings: 25yo male with pre-existing SVT died from multi-organ failure 2 days after ingesting 2.5g ibogaine for heroin cessation; unsupervised setting; highlights pre-existing cardiac conditions as critical risk factor

papers.title: Anti-addiction drug ibogaine inhibits voltage-gated ionic currents: A study to assess the drug's cardiac ion channel profile
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine IC₅₀: hERG 4 μM, hNaᵥ1.5 142 μM, hCaᵥ1.2 163 μM. Free plasma 1–11 μM at therapeutic dose. Computer model predicts human AP prolongation despite Caᵥ1.2 counteraction. 18-MC hERG IC₅₀ 15 μM.
contraindications: Hypokalaemia (exacerbates hERG blockade and reduces channel surface density)
papers.dosing_range: IC₅₀: hERG 4 μM, hNav1.5 142 μM, hCav1.2 163 μM; 18-MC hERG 15 μM — in vitro concentrations

papers.title: Fatal Case of a 27-Year-Old Male After Taking Iboga in Withdrawal Treatment: GC-MS/MS Determination of Ibogaine and Ibogamine in Iboga Roots and Postmortem Biological Material
papers.key_findings: FATALITY CASE: 27yo male died ~12h after iboga root ingestion during withdrawal treatment. Femoral blood ibogaine 1.27 μg/mL. Co-ingestion with methadone and diazepam — triple QT-prolongation risk.
contraindications: Unsupervised treatment setting without medical monitoring
Unknown or unverified dose of crude iboga root bark
papers.dosing_range: Unknown; claimed 'teaspoon' (~108–144 mg ibogaine from 1.5–2g powder at 7.2%), but blood levels suggest substantially higher dose (possibly >20g powder)

papers.title: Ibogaine related sudden death: A case report
papers.key_findings: 52yo alcoholic male died 12–24h post-ibogaine (blood 2 mg/L). Autopsy: liver cirrhosis (>90% fatty infiltration), 40–45% coronary occlusion, myocardial ischaemia. Death classified cardiac, ibogaine-related.
papers.journal: Journal of Forensic and Legal Medicine

papers.title: Ibogaine Treatment for Substance Dependence: Legality, Social Geography, and Efficacy
papers.key_findings: COMPREHENSIVE REVIEW: Global ibogaine medical subculture development, legal geography (Mexico/Canada/NZ vs US Schedule I), sustainability concerns, Baja California long-term outcomes research.
papers.journal: II Congreso Internacional de medicina tradicional y salud pública

papers.title: Ibogaine Effect on Cocaine Craving and Use in Dependent Patients - A Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Pilot Study
papers.key_findings: Single 1800mg ibogaine dose significantly reduced cocaine craving (MCCS 7.4→1.3 at 72h, p<0.0001) and positive urine samples vs placebo over 24 weeks in N=20 RCT.
contraindications: Current alcohol or other drug dependence (other than nicotine)
Significant cardiovascular conditions or current cardiac condition
Lifetime diagnosis of schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, or other psychotic disorders
papers.dosing_range: 1800 mg single dose (dried ibogaine extract, 75% purity, encapsulated with excipients)
papers.journal: Jacobs Journal of Addiction and Therapy

papers.title: Treating drug dependence with the aid of ibogaine: A retrospective study
papers.key_findings: Brazilian retrospective (n=75, 134 sessions): 61% abstinent, median 5.5mo (single) vs 8.4mo (multiple tx, p<0.001). Zero serious AEs or fatalities. First systematic non-opioid dependence data.
contraindications: Active substance use (30–60 day abstinence period required prior to administration)

papers.title: Mechanism of hERG Channel Block by the Psychoactive Indole Alkaloid Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine blocks hERG channels from cytosolic side via open/inactivated-state binding at Y652/F656 residues; IC₅₀ ~1-4 μM; F656A mutation reduces potency 50-fold; inactivation-deficient mutant equally sensitive.
contraindications: Concomitant use of other hERG-blocking drugs
Conditions favouring intracellular acidosis (increases ibogaine trapping and hERG block)
papers.dosing_range: IC₅₀ ~1–4 μM (hERG wild-type); standard test concentration 3 μM — in vitro
papers.journal: Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics

papers.title: Cardiac Arrest After Ibogaine Ingestion
authors: van der Voort, P. H. J.
papers.key_findings: Cardiac arrest from VF in healthy 26M ~5h post 2400 mg ibogaine (32.4 mg/kg); QTc max 663 ms at 18h; serum ibogaine 948 µg/L; permanent neurological deficits from post-anoxic encephalopathy

papers.title: Herbal seizures - atypical symptoms after ibogaine intoxication: a case report
papers.key_findings: SEIZURE CASE: 22yo male, extreme 38g dose triggered generalised tonic-clonic seizures. Suggests ibogaine proconvulsive at high doses. Treated with midazolam/levetiracetam.
contraindications: High-dose iboga root bark ingestion (proconvulsive at high doses)
papers.dosing_range: 38g root bark (~35mg/kg estimated at 7% ibogaine content)

papers.title: Deconstructing the Iboga Alkaloid Skeleton: Potentiation of FGF2-induced Glial Cell Line-Derived Neurotrophic Factor Release by a Novel Compound
papers.key_findings: Novel acyclic iboga analogue XL-008 potentiates FGF2-induced GDNF release >2-fold potency and >3-fold efficacy in C6 glioma cells via MEK/PI3K pathways. Sames lab, Columbia.

papers.title: Influence of CYP2D6 Activity on the Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics of a Single 20 mg Dose of Ibogaine in Healthy Volunteers
papers.key_findings: CYP2D6 CRITICAL: Paroxetine pretreatment (CYP2D6 inhibition) doubled active moiety exposure. Recommends genotyping + halved doses for CYP2D6 poor metabolisers. 20mg safe in healthy volunteers.

papers.title: Ascending-Dose Study of Noribogaine in Healthy Volunteers: Pharmacokinetics, Pharmacodynamics, Safety, and Tolerability
papers.key_findings: First-in-human noribogaine Phase I: 3-60mg doses safe, well-tolerated. T½=28-49h, high Vd=1417-3086L, dose-linear PK. No mu-opioid agonist effects. Establishes human safety profile.

papers.title: A Case of QT Prolongation and Torsades de Pointes Caused by Ibogaine Toxicity
papers.key_findings: 39yo male: QTc 640→730ms (day 2)→400ms (day 7) after 7g oral ibogaine. 8 episodes polymorphic VT/TdP, normal electrolytes. Suppressed by IV Mg 2g + transvenous overdrive pacing at 80bpm.
aliases: Ibogaine QT Prolongation TdP Case Oxford

papers.title: The Anti-Addiction Drug Ibogaine and the Heart: A Delicate Relation
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive cardiac review: hERG IC₅₀ ~4 μM overlaps therapeutic range; 22 fatalities tabulated with 100% hypokalemia when tested; noribogaine half-life (28–49 h) extends cardiac risk beyond acute phase.

papers.title: Mania following use of ibogaine: A case series
papers.key_findings: SAFETY ALERT: 3 cases de novo mania post-ibogaine in patients with no prior bipolar history. Naranjo scale: probable (2 cases) and possible (1). First published reports of ibogaine-induced mania.
contraindications: Personal or family history of bipolar disorder or mania
Concurrent or recent psilocybin use (confounding psychiatric risk)
Unregulated treatment setting without psychiatric monitoring or follow-up
papers.dosing_range: Unknown in all three cases (unregulated settings; doses not reported or verified)

papers.title: Ex vivo effects of ibogaine on the activity of antioxidative enzymes in human erythrocytes
papers.key_findings: Ex vivo ibogaine (10-20μM) elevates SOD1 and GR in human erythrocytes, increases plasma ATP dose-dependently, stabilises SOD1 against H₂O₂ — pro-antioxidant via energy metabolism.
papers.dosing_range: 10–20 µM ibogaine HCl (human erythrocytes ex vivo)

papers.title: Internet-purchased ibogaine toxicity confirmed with serum, urine, and product content levels
papers.key_findings: Acute ibogaine toxicity: peak serum 377 ng/mL, QTc 527 ms (transient), non-linear elimination (t½ 67–162 min), estimated 3.8 g ingested from internet-purchased capsules (479 mg/capsule).
papers.dosing_range: ~3.8 g oral (8 capsules × 479 mg, over <6 hours)

papers.title: hERG Blockade by Iboga Alkaloids
papers.key_findings: LANDMARK hERG DATA: Whole-cell patch clamp IC₅₀ values - ibogaine 4.09μM, noribogaine 2.86μM, voacangine 2.25μM. 18-MC >50μM (12× safer). Noribogaine's extended T½ may explain delayed cardiac events.
papers.dosing_range: 10–25 mg/kg (typical clinical range); IC₅₀ overlaps therapeutic plasma concentrations (2.4 μM ibogaine, 3.2 μM noribogaine at 10 mg/kg)

papers.title: Ibogaine and addiction in the animal model, a systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.key_findings: SYRCLE META-ANALYSIS: 27 studies show ibogaine reduces drug self-administration especially in first 24h. No effect on CPP. Motor impairment and cerebellar toxicity noted. Warrants clinical trials with monitoring.

papers.title: Remission of Severe Opioid Use Disorder with Ibogaine: A Case Report
papers.key_findings: Treatment-refractory OUD patient (19yr history) achieved 18-month abstinence after 4-day ibogaine HCl treatment (32 mg/kg total). Mild bradycardia observed (51–67 bpm). Patient attributed success to transformative experience over withdrawal relief.

papers.title: Effects of low dose ibogaine on subjective mood state and psychological performance
papers.key_findings: Single 20 mg ibogaine had no effect on mood or most cognitive functions in 21 healthy males; only Simon Congruent RT showed concentration-response association with active moiety (adjusted p=0.016).

papers.title: Ascending Single-Dose, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Safety Study of Noribogaine in Opioid-Dependent Patients
papers.key_findings: First-in-patient RCT of noribogaine (60-180mg) in 27 opioid-dependent patients. Concentration-dependent QTcI prolongation (0.17 ms/ng/mL); mean ΔΔQTcI 16/28/42 ms at 60/120/180mg. t½ 24-30h, dose-linear PK.
contraindications: Risk factors for torsades de pointes

papers.title: Switching Opioid-Dependent Patients From Methadone to Morphine: Safety, Tolerability, and Methadone Pharmacokinetics
papers.key_findings: 7-day methadone-to-morphine switch protocol: 27/27 completed with no dropouts. SR morphine at 5.2× methadone dose adequate. Methadone t½ 59h; 91% cleared in 7 days. No safety issues; no QTcF >500ms.
papers.dosing_range: SR morphine 4–5× methadone dose (mean 311 mg/day); clonidine 200 μg/day patch PRN

papers.title: How toxic is ibogaine?
papers.key_findings: Reviews 27 ibogaine fatalities and 8 cardiac case reports (QTc 460–730ms) in patients WITHOUT pre-existing conditions. CYP2D6 polymorphism affects toxicity. Noribogaine at least as cardiotoxic as ibogaine.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiovascular conditions (cardiomyopathy, prior infarction, coronary stenosis)
QT interval prolongation or family history of cardiac-rhythm abnormalities
CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (requires ≥50% dose reduction)
Concurrent medications metabolised by CYP2D6 (e.g., paroxetine doubles active moiety)
Concurrent buprenorphine use (CYP3A4 competition slows ibogaine clearance)
Concurrent use of other QT-prolonging substances
Unsupervised or limited medical supervision settings
papers.dosing_range: 10–25 mg/kg therapeutic; toxicity cases typically >2g; Lambarène 8mg/tablet historical

papers.title: Oral noribogaine shows high brain uptake and anti-withdrawal effects not associated with place preference in rodents
papers.key_findings: Oral noribogaine reduced morphine withdrawal 88% (ED50=13mg/kg), brain/blood ratio 7±1, no conditioned place preference. Metabolite mediates ibogaine's anti-withdrawal effects.

papers.title: Ibogaine-associated cardiac arrest and death: case report and review of the literature
papers.key_findings: Fatal case: 40yo used 4g ibogaine + 2g 'booster' for heroin detox. Found in asystole 8h later. Brain death from cardiac arrest. Reviews literature on ibogaine cardiotoxicity. Warns of unregulated internet sourcing.
contraindications: Unknown dose or purity of ibogaine

papers.title: Ibogaine for treating drug dependence. What is a safe dose?
papers.key_findings: Calculates theoretical safe oral dose of 0.87 mg/kg from animal NOAEL with safety factors; clinical doses of 6–30 mg/kg overlap toxic ranges; hERG IC50 values match therapeutic plasma concentrations
contraindications: Medical comorbidities associated with long-term drug use
papers.dosing_range: 0.87 mg/kg theoretical safe oral dose; clinical use 6–30 mg/kg oral

papers.title: Subjective effectiveness of ibogaine treatment for problematic opioid consumption: Short- and long-term outcomes and current psychological functioning
papers.key_findings: n=88 opioid patients (Crossroads, Mexico 2012-2015). 80% reported withdrawal elimination/drastic reduction. 30% never used opioids again; 54% of abstainers sustained ≥1yr. 41% sustained abstinence ≥6mo at survey.
contraindications: Organic brain disease or history of severe TBI

papers.title: The antiaddictive effects of ibogaine: A systematic literature review of human studies
papers.key_findings: Systematic review of 8 human studies: 7 open-label case series suggest single ibogaine dose reduces opioid withdrawal within 24h lasting weeks-months; 1 noribogaine RCT (n=27) found no significant effect.
contraindications: Concomitant opiate/opioid use during or immediately after ibogaine
Use of drugs of unknown purity in uncontrolled settings

papers.title: Examination of the Phenomenology of the Ibogaine Treatment Experience: Role of Altered States of Consciousness and Psychedelic Experiences
papers.key_findings: 5d-ASC scores higher than psilocybin across all dimensions; VRS and G-ASC correlated with perceived change; 92% reported craving reduction at 4 weeks; ASCs related to self-reported change

papers.title: Toxicokinetics of ibogaine and noribogaine in a patient with prolonged multiple cardiac arrhythmias after ingestion of internet purchased ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine, not ibogaine, drives prolonged cardiac toxicity. QTc 647 ms with TdP. Noribogaine t½ elimination 2540 h vs ibogaine 228 h. k₁₂/k₂₁ ratio 21.5 for noribogaine implies deep tissue sequestration.
papers.dosing_range: 1400 mg oral (repeated doses over 12 hours, internet-sourced capsules)

papers.title: The Iboga Alkaloids
papers.key_findings: 117 new iboga alkaloids (2000–2016) from 14 species; ibogaine is weak MOR antagonist not agonist; 18-MC selectively targets α3β4 nAChR with no SERT/NMDA affinity; no scalable ibogaine synthesis exists
papers.journal: Progress in the Chemistry of Organic Natural Products

papers.title: Ibogaine treatment outcomes for opioid dependence from a twelve-month follow-up observational study
papers.key_findings: Single ibogaine treatment (mean 31.4 mg/kg) reduced ASI drug use score 81% at 12mo (p=0.002, n=8). BDI-II depression scores fell from 22.1 to 4.4 (p<0.001). 75% negative urine at 12mo. 1 fatality during treatment.
papers.journal: The American Journal of Drug and Alcohol Abuse

papers.title: Anti-addiction Drug Ibogaine Prolongs the Action Potential in Human Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell-Derived Cardiomyocytes
papers.key_findings: First proof ibogaine/noribogaine prolong action potential in human cardiomyocytes (hiPS-CM). Therapeutic concentrations retard repolarisation. Noribogaine's long half-life explains delayed cardiac events days after ingestion.

papers.title: Detoxification from methadone using low, repeated, and increasing doses of ibogaine: A case report
papers.key_findings: PROTOCOL INNOVATION: First documented low, multiple, ascending dose approach (150-600mg) alternating with tapering methadone. 47yo female, 17yr MMT successfully detoxified. No serious AEs, QTc within limits.
contraindications: QTc prolongation risk even at low doses — medical supervision required
Bradycardia risk — HR dropped from 85 to 53 bpm at 400-500 mg doses
papers.dosing_range: 5 ascending doses (150, 300, 400, 500, 600 mg oral) alternating with halving methadone doses over 6 weeks

papers.title: Phytochemical characterization of Tabernanthe iboga root bark and its effects on dysfunctional metabolism and cognitive performance in high-fat-fed C57BL/6J mice
papers.key_findings: T.iboga root bark phytochemistry + metabolic/cognitive effects in high-fat diet mice. IPHAMETRA Gabon + Oregon State collaboration. Characterises alkaloid profile and metabolic effects.
papers.dosing_range: T. iboga root bark extract (mouse, oral gavage)

papers.title: A case report SPECT study and theoretical rationale for the sequential administration of ibogaine and 5-MeO-DMT in the treatment of alcohol use disorder
papers.key_findings: SPECT case: Sequential ibogaine (17.9 mg/kg) + 5-MeO-DMT in veteran with AUD. Increased perfusion bilateral caudate/putamen/insula/cerebellum post-treatment. Alcohol cessation at 1 mo, partial return at 2 mo.
papers.dosing_range: 17.9 mg/kg ibogaine HCl (1550 mg total, split dose); 50 mg bufotoxin (~5–7 mg 5-MeO-DMT, vaporised)

papers.title: Treatment of Opioid Use Disorder with Ibogaine: Detoxification and Drug Use Outcomes
papers.key_findings: Prospective observational study (N=30): ibogaine reduced SOWS from 31.0 to 14.0 (p<.001); 50% opioid-free at 1 month; ASIC Drug Use improved at all time points to 12 months; methadone exposure predicted worse outcome.
papers.journal: The American Journal of Drug and Alcohol Abuse
aliases: First prospective ibogaine OUD outcomes study

papers.title: A phenomenological investigation into the lived experience of ibogaine and its potential to treat opioid use disorders
papers.key_findings: 10 opioid-dependent participants reported ibogaine attenuated withdrawal (100%), eliminated cravings (100%), increased self-acceptance (100%), and induced spiritual outlook (70%)
contraindications: Opioid use within 72 hours of ibogaine ingestion
papers.dosing_range: 15–20 mg/kg therapeutic dose referenced; LD50 264 mg/kg

papers.title: Ibogaine as a Treatment for Substance Misuse: Potential Benefits and Practical Dangers
papers.key_findings: Most comprehensive fatality review: 33 ibogaine-associated deaths (1990–2018); mean age 39.5 years; 78% opioid detoxification; cardiac disease in ≥6 cases; co-intoxicants in ≥12; 1:427 death-to-treatment ratio.
contraindications: Pulmonary embolism risk factors (immobility, IV drug use history)
papers.dosing_range: variable/unknown — retrospective review of 33 fatalities; therapeutic range 500–1000 mg (10–25 mg/kg); post-mortem blood levels 0.24–9.3 mg/L
aliases: Progress in Brain Research ibogaine chapter

papers.title: A Mixed-Method Analysis of Persisting Effects Associated with Positive Outcomes Following Ibogaine Detoxification
papers.key_findings: 81% treatment responders (n=73 opioid users). Large persisting gains in gratitude, meaning, inner peace, sacredness (d=0.89–1.27). Integration was top challenge (23%).

papers.title: Ibogaine Acute Administration in Rats Promotes Wakefulness, Long-Lasting REM Sleep Suppression, and a Distinctive Motor Profile
papers.key_findings: SLEEP ARCHITECTURE: Ibogaine promotes wakefulness, robust long-lasting REM suppression, dose-dependent motor profile (tremor, flat posture at high dose). No head-shake (5HT2A marker). Uruguay polysomnography.

papers.title: Hallucinogen Persisting Perception Disorder After Ibogaine Treatment for Opioid Dependence
papers.key_findings: First reported HPPD after ibogaine (10 mg/kg HCl). Onset 48h post-dose, persisted ~5 weeks. One EMDR session produced substantial relief. QTc 420→516 ms. Patient relapsed to heroin.

papers.title: Psychedelics Promote Structural and Functional Neural Plasticity
papers.key_findings: LSD, DMT, psilocin, noribogaine promote neuritogenesis, spinogenesis, synaptogenesis via TrkB/mTOR/5-HT₂A. LSD EC₅₀=0.41nM. Ibogaine inactive; noribogaine active. Effects comparable to ketamine.

papers.title: Changes in Withdrawal and Craving Scores in Participants Undergoing Opioid Detoxification Utilizing Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Retrospective chart review (N=50): COWS scores ↓ from 8.2 to 3.3, SOWS 20.5 to 10.0, BSCS 6.6 to 1.9 at 48h post-ibogaine (all p<0.01); 78% no clinical withdrawal, 79% minimal cravings; η²=0.54–0.69.
contraindications: Severe psychiatric conditions (psychotic spectrum, bipolar I, eating disorders)
Pulmonary embolism or deep vein thrombosis
Organic brain disease or severe TBI
Alcohol, amphetamines, cocaine, or psychiatric medications within 1 week

papers.title: Ibogaine Detoxification Transitions Opioid and Cocaine Abusers Between Dependence and Abstinence: Clinical Observations and Treatment Outcomes
papers.key_findings: MAJOR COHORT: n=191 opioid/cocaine dependent patients (St. Kitts clinic). Ibogaine (10-25mg/kg) effectively blocked withdrawal symptoms, reduced drug craving, improved mood. Safe in dose range studied with medical monitoring.

papers.title: The Effects of Ibogaine on Uterine Smooth Muscle Contractions: Relation to the Activity of Antioxidant Enzymes
papers.key_findings: Low ibogaine concentrations stimulate uterine contractions; higher doses inhibit. Concentration-dependent effects linked to ATP depletion and altered redox homeostasis. SOD1/GSH-Px/CAT activity changes.
papers.dosing_range: 1–60 mg/l ibogaine (isolated uterine tissue, cumulative dosing)

papers.title: Understanding the Healing Potential of Ibogaine through a Comparative and Interpretive Phenomenology of the Visionary Experience
papers.key_findings: Comparative phenomenological analysis through psychoanalytic vs constructivist lenses. Healing via culturally-shaped imaginal journey to unspoilt state. Argues secular framework vital for medical legitimacy.

papers.title: Cardiac arrest after ibogaine intoxication
papers.key_findings: Extreme overdose survival case: 65-70mg/kg ibogaine (5.6g total) - highest survived dose documented. Presented with ventricular flutter (270 bpm), massive QTc prolongation requiring defibrillation. QT normalised after 7 days.

papers.title: DARK Classics in Chemical Neuroscience: Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: ACS DARK Classics review covering ibogaine chemistry, pharmacology, and 18-MC development. α3β4 nAChR proposed as primary anti-addiction target. Comprehensive receptor binding table across 20+ targets.

papers.title: An Analytical Study of Iboga Alkaloids in Products from Treatment Providers
papers.key_findings: CRITICAL: n=16 products from 5 vendors - purity ranged 0-73.4% ibogaine. One sample contained ZERO ibogaine. Extreme variability makes dose calculation impossible without analysis.
contraindications: Unverified ibogaine product purity (0-73% range found across vendors)

papers.title: Ibogaine and Subjective Experience: Transformative States and Psychopharmacotherapy in the Treatment of Opioid Use Disorder
papers.key_findings: 43% of 42 ibogaine-treated OUD participants achieved complete mystical experience on SCQ; qualitative themes of confrontation, remorse, letting go, and transformation emerged
contraindications: Substance use disorders other than opioid dependence
Physical or psychosocial problems preventing compliance with study requirements
papers.dosing_range: 22.5 ± 10.1 mg/kg HCl (Mexico, n=26); 31.4 ± 7.6 mg/kg HCl (NZ, n=14); 9.9 ± 7.8 mg/kg HCl + 18.7 ± 17.3 mg/kg crude rootbark extract (Mexico, n=4)

papers.title: Serotonin transporter–ibogaine complexes illuminate mechanisms of inhibition and transport
papers.key_findings: NATURE LANDMARK: First cryo-EM SERT-ibogaine structures showing outward-open, occluded, inward-open conformations. Defines transport mechanism and ibogaine non-competitive inhibition at molecular level.

papers.title: Ibogaine Consumption with Seizure-Like Episodes, QTc-Prolongation, and Captured Cardiac Dysrhythmias
papers.key_findings: Case of QTc 788ms and torsade de pointes after 2g internet-purchased ibogaine; terminated by IV magnesium; highlights unmonitored self-treatment risks

papers.title: The Iboga Enigma: The Chemistry and Neuropharmacology of Iboga Alkaloids and Related Analogs
papers.key_findings: COMPREHENSIVE CHEMISTRY: 120+ species, biosynthesis pathways (CorS, CS, TS enzymes), total synthesis strategies, SAR for analogue development. UC Davis Olson lab review establishing synthetic foundations.

papers.title: Ibogaine Administration Modifies GDNF and BDNF Expression in Brain Regions Involved in Mesocorticolimbic and Nigral Dopaminergic Circuits
papers.key_findings: GDNF/BDNF MECHANISM: Ibogaine 40mg/kg upregulates GDNF in VTA (24h), BDNF in NAcc/SN/PFC. Dose-dependent, region-specific neurotrophin expression - mechanistic basis for lasting anti-addictive effects.

papers.title: Ibogaine therapy for addiction: Consumer views from online fora
papers.key_findings: Online fora serve as DIY research hubs for ibogaine seekers; users value both pharmacological 'brain reset' and visionary psychological insights; clinic preferred for safety but cost drives home use.

papers.title: Tropane-Based Ibogaine Analog Rescues Folding-Deficient Serotonin and Dopamine Transporters
papers.key_findings: Tropane-based ibogaine analogue (9b) rescued 6/13 disease-associated DAT mutants causing infantile Parkinsonism/dystonia. Novel pharmacochaperone lead for DTDS with improved therapeutic index.

papers.title: Ibogaine and Noribogaine in the Treatment of Drug Dependency: A State of the Art Review about Safety and Efficacy
papers.key_findings: MSc dissertation systematic review (2000-2020): ibogaine/noribogaine safety and efficacy in substance use disorders. Confirms anti-addictive properties, cardiac risks, need for medical supervision.

papers.title: Psychedelic Treatment for Trauma-Related Psychological and Cognitive Impairment Among US Special Operations Forces Veterans
papers.key_findings: Retrospective n=51 SOF Veterans (2017-2019): Ibogaine+5-MeO-DMT showed very large reductions in PTSD (PCL-5, d=3.6), depression (PHQ-2, d=3.7), anxiety (GAD-2, d=3.1), suicidal ideation (d=1.9), cognitive impairment (d=2.8).
contraindications: Current or past psychotic spectrum disorders

papers.title: Study of the Acute Toxicity of the Aqueous Extract of Tabernanthe iboga (Baill.) (Apocynacea) on Wistar Rats
papers.key_findings: Aqueous T. iboga root bark extract LD50=1442.6 mg/kg oral in Wistar rats, classifying whole extract as low toxicity (Hodge-Sterner). First LD50 study of total Gabonese iboga extract.
papers.dosing_range: LD50=1442.6 mg/kg; LD0=784.71 mg/kg; LD100=2000 mg/kg (aqueous root bark extract, oral, Wistar rats)
papers.journal: Asian Journal of Science and Technology

papers.title: The Ibogaine Experience: A Qualitative Study on the Acute Subjective Effects of Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Grounded-theory study (n=20) identified 8 categories and 10 subcategories of acute ibogaine effects; observer quality and self-psychoanalysis enhancement distinguish ibogaine from classical serotonergic psychedelics.
papers.dosing_range: 6–28 mg/kg oral (i-HCl, TA, root bark, or combinations; see dosing table)

papers.title: A Single Administration of the Atypical Psychedelic Ibogaine or its Metabolite Noribogaine Induces an Antidepressant-like Effect in Rats
papers.key_findings: Single ibogaine or noribogaine dose produces antidepressant-like effect in rat FST; both compounds required simultaneously in brain, suggesting polypharmacological mechanism beyond SERT inhibition
papers.dosing_range: 20 and 40 mg/kg IP (ibogaine HCl and noribogaine HCl); 1 and 5 mg/kg IV (ibogaine HCl)

papers.title: Novel treatment of opioid use disorder using ibogaine and iboga in two adults
papers.key_findings: Two Canadian OUD cases: Case 1 achieved total opioid abstinence (3yr+) after single treatment. Case 2 used multiple iboga sessions to taper off OAT, maintained 2yr+ abstinence. Demonstrates treatment versatility.
papers.dosing_range: variable - flood dose protocol over 6 days

papers.title: Death due to consumption of ibogaine: case report
papers.key_findings: 27yo heroin addict died 5-12h post oral iboga powder (online-purchased); blood ibogaine 3.26 mg/L; morphine/codeine co-detected; no cardiac pathology on histology
papers.dosing_range: unknown (iboga root bark powder, blood ibogaine 3.26 mg/L)

papers.title: The Holy Spirit of Iboga and a Contemporary Perspective on Africa's Spiritual Renaissance: Focus on Gabonese Bwiti Tradition
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive ethnographic analysis of Gabonese Bwiti tradition tracing origins from Babongo Pygmies through modern syncretic forms; positions Bwiti as potential catalyst for African spiritual renaissance

papers.title: A Non-Hallucinogenic Psychedelic Analog with Therapeutic Potential
papers.key_findings: TBG: non-hallucinogenic ibogaine analog, ~100× lower hERG inhibition (IC₅₀ ~100 μM vs 1 μM), promotes neural plasticity via 5-HT2A, reduces alcohol/heroin-seeking in rodents with effects lasting 12–14 days

papers.title: Novel Class of Psychedelic Iboga Alkaloids Disrupts Opioid Addiction States
papers.key_findings: Columbia/Sames lab oxa-iboga alkaloids: synthetic variants maintain anti-addictive efficacy while reducing cardiac liability. Novel class development for safer analogues.

papers.title: Safety of ibogaine administration in detoxification of opioid-dependent individuals: a descriptive open-label observational study
papers.key_findings: Dutch hospital study (n=14). QTc prolongation avg 95ms (range 29-146ms); 50% reached >500ms. Prolongation >450ms lasted >24h in 6 patients. No torsade. Severe but reversible ataxia in all. 11/14 did not return to morphine.

papers.title: Ibogaine: To Have or Have Not. A review of the historical, legal and ethical implications of returning control of ibogaine to drug users
papers.key_findings: Posthumous Lotsof essay on user control & drug policy: DEA officer's 50% demand reduction estimate; pharma/NIDA opposition mechanisms; ibogaine underground railroad emergence; harm reduction vs demand reduction debate.
aliases: Lotsof To Have or Have Not

papers.title: Serotonergic Psychedelics in Neural Plasticity
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine, not ibogaine, increases dendritic arbor complexity via 5-HT2A. Ibogaine neurotoxic at ≥50 mg/kg (Purkinje cell degeneration). TBG: non-hallucinogenic, non-cardiotoxic ibogaine analogue promoting spine formation.

papers.title: Evaluating the toxicity and therapeutic potential of ibogaine in the treatment of chronic opioid abuse
papers.key_findings: DemeRx expert opinion review. CYP2D6 metabolism, noribogaine half-life 24-30h. Reviews benefit-risk ratio for opioid withdrawal. Discusses hERG concerns and drug development barriers.
papers.journal: Expert Opinion on Drug Metabolism & Toxicology

papers.title: The adverse events of ibogaine in humans: an updated systematic review of the literature (2015–2020)
papers.key_findings: PRISMA systematic review of 18 studies (2015–2020): QTc prolongation most common AE (max 788ms), TdP in 5 cases, 1 fatality. Extends Alper 2012/Koenig 2015 series.
contraindications: CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (2× increased ibogaine/noribogaine exposure)
Concurrent use of CYP2D6 inhibitors (e.g., paroxetine)
Use of unstandardised root bark preparations (ibogaine content 0.6–11.2%)
papers.dosing_range: 725 mg ibogaine HCl to 38 g dried root bark across reviewed studies; clinical trials used 20 mg ibogaine or 60–180 mg noribogaine

papers.title: A New Approach Methodology (NAM) for the Prediction of (Nor)Ibogaine-Induced Cardiotoxicity in Humans
papers.key_findings: PBK model predicts ibogaine QTc BMDL₁₀ of 97mg/70kg; noribogaine dominates in vivo cardiotoxicity due to high plasma levels and lower protein binding
contraindications: Doses above BMDL₁₀ of ~97 mg ibogaine (70 kg body weight) predicted to cause significant QTc prolongation
CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (up to 43-fold higher ibogaine concentrations)
Concurrent CYP2D6 inhibitor use (26-fold higher ibogaine Cmax reported)
Clinical doses (420–2100 mg) are 4–21-fold above predicted BMDL₁₀ for QTc prolongation
papers.dosing_range: BMDL₁₀: 97 mg ibogaine / 70 kg (QTc onset); clinical range discussed: 420–2100 mg (6–30 mg/kg)

papers.title: A narrative review of the pharmacological, cultural and psychological literature on ibogaine
papers.key_findings: COMPREHENSIVE: Narrative review covering pharmacological (NMDA antagonist), cultural (Bwiti worldview), psychological (holotropic consciousness theory) aspects. Questions oneirophrenic classification; proposes ibogaine experience as holotropic state.

papers.title: Ibogaine microdosing in a patient with bipolar depression: a case report
papers.key_findings: First published ibogaine microdosing case for bipolar depression. 8mg/day for 60 days. Sustained improvement in depression, anxiety, hopelessness through day 90. No manic switch. Brazilian case.

papers.title: Ibogaine and Their Analogs as Therapeutics for Neurological and Psychiatric Disorders
papers.key_findings: ACS Med Chem Letters patent highlight: ibogaine analogue development for neurological/psychiatric disorders. IP landscape review.

papers.title: A Systematic Literature Review of Clinical Trials and Therapeutic Applications of Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Basel systematic review (n=705 across 24 studies): efficacy for SUD withdrawal/craving; depression/trauma benefits; 2 fatalities in reviewed studies; 18-MC & tabernanthalog as safer alternatives.

papers.title: Addiction Pharmacology and Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Pharmacist's synthesis framing ibogaine risk as manageable with screening; noribogaine QTc dose-response (16–42 ms); 18 contraindications; CYP2D6 inhibitors double exposure; fatal range 4.5–29 mg/kg
contraindications: Medications known to prolong QTc interval
Concurrent opioid, cocaine, or other drug use (re-sensitisation risk)
Acute alcohol or benzodiazepine withdrawal (seizure risk)
papers.dosing_range: Therapeutic: 8–31 mg/kg (observational studies); estimated safe dose 0.87 mg/kg; fatal range 4.5–29 mg/kg; noribogaine dose-ranging 60/120/180 mg

papers.title: Drug Transporters ABCB1 (P-gp) and OATP, but not Drug-Metabolizing Enzyme CYP3A4, Affect the Pharmacokinetics of the Psychoactive Alkaloid Ibogaine and its Metabolites
papers.key_findings: ABCB1/ABCG2 modestly restrict ibogaine oral availability (2.3× AUC) and brain penetration (1.5×). Human OATP1B1/1B3 and CYP3A4 have minimal impact — reducing drug interaction risk.
papers.dosing_range: 10 mg/kg oral (ibogaine HCl in DMSO:saline 1:9, v/v) — mouse study

papers.title: Underground Ibogaine Use for the Treatment of Substance Use Disorders: A Qualitative Analysis of Subjective Experiences
papers.key_findings: Drug & Alcohol Review qualitative study: underground ibogaine use for SUD treatment; subjective experience themes; unregulated setting context.

papers.title: What We Have Gained from Ibogaine: α3β4 Nicotinic Acetylcholine Receptor Inhibitors as Treatments for Substance Use Disorders
papers.key_findings: RETROSPECTIVE ADD: Review of ibogaine and α3β4 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor inhibitors as treatments for substance use disorders.

papers.title: Ibogaína: un psicodélico atípico con potencial antiadictivo
papers.key_findings: Spanish-language review for psychiatrists covering ibogaine pharmacology, anti-addictive mechanisms (GDNF, neuroplasticity), clinical evidence summary, and cardiac safety. Includes comprehensive clinical trials table.

papers.title: (+)-Catharanthine and (-)-18-methoxycoronaridine induce antidepressant-like activity in mice by differently recruiting serotonergic and norepinephrinergic neurotransmission
papers.key_findings: Eur J Pharmacol: (+)-catharanthine and (-)-18-MC antidepressant-like activity in mice. Differential SERT/NET recruitment. FST/TST at 40mg/kg. Coronaridine congener mechanisms.

papers.title: A review of the mechanisms involved in the neuroprotection and neurotoxicity of Iboga alkaloids
papers.key_findings: Dual neuroprotective/neurotoxic review from Gabon (IPHAMETRA/CENAREST). Crude extract LD₅₀ = 1442.571 mg/kg oral; ibogaine LD₅₀ = 263–327 mg/kg; noribogaine LD₅₀ = 630 mg/kg. σ₂ receptor affinity correlates with neurotoxicity.

papers.title: The Effect of Ibogaine on Cognitive Functioning
papers.key_findings: First prospective study of ibogaine (~20mg/kg) on neuropsychological performance in SOV with TBI; significant improvements in processing speed, cognitive inhibition, and verbal memory at 1-month follow-up
papers.journal: Journal of the International Neuropsychological Society

papers.title: Open-label study of consecutive ibogaine and 5-MeO-DMT assisted-therapy for trauma-exposed male Special Operations Forces Veterans: prospective data from a clinical program in Mexico
papers.key_findings: SOFV prospective n=86: Ibogaine 10mg/kg + 5-MeO-DMT 50mg over 3 days. Large improvements in PTSD (η²=0.414), depression, anxiety, insomnia, post-concussive symptoms at 1-month. Effects sustained to 6-months.
papers.journal: American Journal of Drug and Alcohol Abuse

papers.title: Ibogaine reduced severe neuropathic pain associated with a case of brachial plexus nerve root avulsion
papers.key_findings: FIRST DOCUMENTED: 53yo male, 20-year intractable brachial plexus nerve root avulsion pain. High-dose inpatient + low-dose outpatient ibogaine protocol achieved sustained pain reduction and functional improvement.
papers.dosing_range: 15 mg/kg flood + 38 mg/kg saturation protocol; outpatient 3–12 mg/kg

papers.title: Transpersonal Intersubjectivity in Ibogaine Experiences: Three cases
papers.key_findings: Qualitative phenomenological study (n=3 case reports) examining transpersonal intersubjectivity in ibogaine experiences - sense of communication with ibogaine itself or entities.

papers.title: Ibogaine Treatment in Combat Veterans Significantly Improves Sleep, Beyond Alleviating Posttraumatic Stress Disorder Symptoms
papers.key_findings: n=30 SOF Veterans with TBI. Ibogaine improved PTSD, depression, anxiety, disability, cognition. Effect sizes d=1.4-4.78. No serious adverse events.

papers.title: Three Decades of Research on the Development of Ibogaine Treatment of Substance Use Disorders: A Scientometric Analysis
papers.key_findings: Scientometric analysis of 1,523 ibogaine publications (1993–2022) across 70 countries; USA produced 59% of output; no human RCTs identified; keyword shift from cocaine/morphine to opioids/psychedelics in third decade.
aliases: Scientometric Analysis Three Decades Ibogaine Research

papers.title: Tripping Outside the Lines: Lessons from Observational Studies of Combination Psychedelic Use Beyond Regulated Clinical Contexts
papers.key_findings: Analysis of combination psychedelic use (including ibogaine+5-MeO-DMT protocols) in unregulated observational contexts, extracting lessons for clinical practice.
papers.journal: American Journal of Drug and Alcohol Abuse

papers.title: Ibogaine – A legacy within the current renaissance of psychedelic therapy
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive historical review by Deborah Mash covering 100+ years ibogaine research. Documents FDA-approved Phase 1 trial (1993, IND39,680) discontinued 1995.

papers.title: Ibogaine/Noribogaine in the Treatment of Substance Use Disorders: A Systematic Review of the Current Literature
papers.key_findings: Systematic review of 31 studies. Meta-analysis shows efficacy for SUD but significant cardiotoxicity. Most deaths in uncontrolled settings. Headache only significant adverse effect vs placebo.

papers.title: Psychedelics reopen the social reward learning critical period
papers.key_findings: All psychedelics reopen social reward learning critical period in mice; ibogaine open state ≥4 weeks (longest tested), proportional to 36-72h human subjective effects; convergent mechanism is ECM remodelling, not 5-HT₂A

papers.title: Main targets of ibogaine and noribogaine associated with its putative anti-addictive effects: A mechanistic overview
papers.key_findings: Updates Popik 1995 review. IBO/NOR modulate ≥8 targets (MOR, KOR, DAT, SERT, NMDA, nAChR α3β4, NTFs, ABC transporters). Advocates polypharmacology paradigm.
