papers.title: Identifying setting factors associated with improved ibogaine safety: a systematic review of clinical studies
papers.key_findings: Systematic review of 12 clinical studies (163 completed participants): zero serious adverse events in controlled settings; all reported fatalities occurred in uncontrolled settings without medical oversight.
contraindications: Administration in uncontrolled settings without medical supervision
Absence of pre-treatment medical screening protocols
Pre-existing hepatic disease (e.g., chronic hepatitis C)
Axis I psychotic disorders (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, psychotic spectrum)
Dissociative disorders or high risk of developing severe mental illness
Personal or family history of bipolar or psychotic disorders
Concomitant use of psychotropic medications without adequate discontinuation
Concurrent use of other substances of abuse
Unknown-purity or uncharacterised ibogaine preparations (root bark, unverified extracts)
Nutritional deficiencies (risk factor for QT prolongation)
QTc prolongation >500ms (emergency magnesium protocol threshold per Knuijver)
papers.dosing_range: Ibogaine HCl 20–800 mg or 8–12 mg/kg; noribogaine HCl 3–180 mg; one study used 1800 mg encapsulated extract (75% purity) — all oral, across 12 reviewed clinical studies
papers.journal: European Archives of Psychiatry and Clinical Neuroscience

papers.title: Guidelines for Establishing Safety in Ayahuasca and Ibogaine Administration in Clinical Settings
papers.key_findings: USP/LEAPS clinical protocols: 72h cardiac monitoring, electrolyte targets K+ 4.5-5.5/Mg 1.5-2.5, medication tapering schedules, 3-person minimum team, trauma-informed approach framework.
contraindications: Personal or family history of psychosis, bipolar disorder, or schizophrenia
Recent cardiac, renal, or hepatic pathology

papers.title: A transcriptomic analysis in mice following a single dose of ibogaine identifies new potential therapeutic targets
papers.key_findings: First transcriptomic analysis of ibogaine effects on gene expression in mouse frontal cortex (4h post-dose, 60mg/kg oral). Upregulated: hormonal pathways (Oxt, Avp), synaptogenesis genes.

papers.title: Noribogaine acute administration in rats promotes wakefulness and suppresses REM sleep
papers.key_findings: Noribogaine promotes wakefulness, reduces SWS, and profoundly suppresses REM sleep in rats (20 and 40 mg/kg IP). Effects stronger than ibogaine, implicating SERT blockade as the shared mechanism.
papers.dosing_range: 20 and 40 mg/kg IP (noribogaine·HCl in warm degassed saline)

papers.title: Risk Assessment of Herbal Preparations Containing Tabernanthe iboga
papers.key_findings: RIVM advises against consumer use of T. iboga; 34 sudden deaths tabulated (from 4.5 mg/kg); QTc prolongation not dose-dependent across individuals; no safe dose derivable
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease (cardiomyopathy, valvular disease, coronary artery disease)
Active neurological disorders (cerebellar dysfunction, organic brain disease, dementia)
Active psychiatric disorders (psychosis, bipolar illness, major depression)
Concurrent use of CYP2D6 inhibitors (e.g. paroxetine)
Concurrent methadone use (extremely long half-life 15–55 hours)
CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (higher ibogaine plasma levels, altered toxicokinetics)
papers.dosing_range: 1–30 mg/kg bw oral (common use); 0.3–55 mg/kg bw (clinical studies); LD₅₀ 263 mg/kg bw mice, 327 mg/kg bw rats; noribogaine LD₅₀ 630 mg/kg bw mice

papers.title: Psychedelic Therapy: A Primer for Primary Care Clinicians—Ibogaine
papers.key_findings: Clinician primer covering safety (33 deaths, cardiotoxicity, Mg protection), OUD efficacy (50-65% craving reduction), veteran PTSD outcomes (86% remission). Only 1 RCT exists.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions (coronary artery sclerosis, hypertension, myocardial infarct, cardiac hypertrophy, dilated cardiomyopathy)
Prolonged baseline QT interval, including drug-induced (antibiotics, antidepressants)
hERG mutations associated with long-QT syndrome
Concurrent long-acting opioids — must switch to short-acting opioids up to two weeks in advance
Poor CYP2D6 metabolism (risk factor for impaired ibogaine clearance; methadone also inhibits CYP2D6)
Motor impairment conditions (Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis — potential contraindication, not yet studied)
Multiple concurrent substance addictions (polysubstance use increases fatality risk)
History of mania or psychosis (3 reported cases, all in unregulated settings)

papers.title: Magnesium–ibogaine therapy in veterans with traumatic brain injuries
papers.key_findings: MISTIC (Nature Medicine), n=30 SOF Veterans/TBI. Effect sizes: WHODAS d=2.20, PTSD d=2.54, Depression d=2.80, Anxiety d=2.13. Mg+Ibo prevented all QTc prolongation. No SAEs.

papers.title: Ibogaine administration following repeated morphine administration upregulates myelination markers CNP and MBP in Sprague Dawley rats
papers.key_findings: First study showing ibogaine upregulates myelination markers (CNP and MBP) in rat internal capsule post-morphine. Novel mechanism: white matter repair may contribute to addiction treatment effects.

papers.title: Oxa-Iboga alkaloids lack cardiac risk and disrupt opioid use in animal models
papers.key_findings: BREAKTHROUGH: Oxa-iboga alkaloids (benzofuran-containing) lack proarrhythmic effects of ibogaine/noribogaine, show superior efficacy in OUD models. Potent KOR agonists with single-dose efficacy.

papers.title: Tabernanthalog Reduces Motivation for Heroin and Alcohol in a Polydrug Use Model
papers.key_findings: Tabernanthalog (TBG, 30mg/kg) efficacy in polydrug heroin+alcohol model. TBG reduced break points for both heroin (p=0.040) and alcohol (p=0.001). Non-hallucinogenic iboga analogue shows promise.

papers.title: Disrupting Substance Use Disorder: The Chemistry of Iboga Alkaloids
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive synthesis review: 20+ total syntheses of iboga alkaloids. Drug leads 18-MC (Phase II), oxa-noribogaine (κ-opioid, no cardiac risk), TBG (non-hallucinogenic, 5-HT2A plasticity)

papers.title: The Nonclassic Psychedelic Ibogaine Disrupts Cognitive Maps
papers.key_findings: Two-photon microscopy: ibogaine (40mg/kg) destabilised cognitive map in retrosplenial cortex. Supports entropy hypothesis - psychedelics disrupt constraining representations, increasing neural signalling entropy.

papers.title: Streamlined screening platforms lead to the discovery of pachysiphine synthase from Tabernanthe iboga
papers.key_findings: Discovery of two T. iboga CYP450 enzymes: pachysiphine synthase (TiPS) and tabersonine-16-hydroxylase (TiT16H) using N. benthamiana leaf disk screening; advances understanding of MIA biosynthesis

papers.title: The pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of ibogaine in opioid use disorder patients
papers.key_findings: First human PK/PD study (n=14): CYP2D6 genotype is MAJOR determinant of ibogaine metabolism - clearance varies >10-fold across activity scores. Critical for personalised dosing.
contraindications: Clinically significant cardiac disease (including ventricular fibrillation, long QT syndrome, syncope history)

papers.title: Ibogain a jeho potenciální využití v léčbě drogových závislostí (Ibogaine and its potential use in the treatment of drug addiction)
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive Czech review covering ibogaine mechanisms (NMDA, opioid, nAChR), CYP2D6 metabolism with sex differences, cardiac hERG risks, and safer analogue 18-MC development.

papers.title: Multiple Episodes of Cardiac Arrest Induced by Treatment With Ibogaine: A Case Report
papers.key_findings: Portuguese case: Multiple cardiac arrests after only 200mg ibogaine (2.6mg/kg test dose). QTc 636ms, torsade de pointes, 3 defibrillations. Demonstrates even low doses can be lethal. No structural heart disease.

papers.title: Ibogaine for the Treatment of Opioid Dependence: From Mechanisms of Action to Clinical Efficacy
papers.key_findings: PhD thesis comprehensively reviewing ibogaine for opioid dependence. Covers mechanisms, pharmacology, clinical efficacy, safety. ICEERS-affiliated. Supervised by Bouso and Colomina.

papers.title: Subjective ibogaine experiences across intersecting social-ecological dimensions
papers.key_findings: First qualitative study of ibogaine experiences across social-ecological dimensions (individual, interpersonal, systems). Consensual Qualitative Research method reveals multi-level impacts on recovery.

papers.title: Ibogaine Induces Cardiotoxic Necrosis in Rats—The Role of Redox Processes
papers.key_findings: First demonstration that oral ibogaine causes dose-dependent myocardial necrosis in rats. Lower dose (1mg/kg): focal necrosis. Higher dose (20mg/kg): diffuse necrosis affecting 60% of myocardium. Non-inflammatory mechanism.

papers.title: Ibogaine induces juvenile-like plasticity and modulates functional and structural regulators of plasticity in the adult mouse visual cortex
papers.key_findings: Single ibogaine dose (40mg/kg) restores juvenile-like plasticity in adult mouse visual cortex by reducing PNNs, PV interneurons, and vGAT—structural/inhibitory brakes on plasticity.

papers.title: Old Dog, New Tricks: Ibogaine and Its Analogs as Potential Neurotherapeutics
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive review of ibogaine pharmacology and synthetic analogues. 18-MC and tabernanthalog show therapeutic potential without cardiotoxicity (hERG IC₅₀ >50μM and 148μM respectively). Texas allocated $50M for ibogaine clinical research.

papers.title: Mystical experiences during magnesium-Ibogaine are associated with improvements in PTSD symptoms in veterans
papers.key_findings: n=30 veterans with TBI; higher MEQ30 predicted larger PTSD reductions at immediate (B=-5.89, p<.001) and 1-month (B=-4.45, p=.007) follow-up. Mystical experience also predicted decreased peak alpha frequency at 1 month. MISTIC protocol.
contraindications: Clinical abnormalities affecting participant safety or study integrity
Risk for suicidal behaviour during study period
Concurrent participation in another clinical trial
papers.dosing_range: Test dose 2–3 mg/kg, total ≤14 mg/kg oral (ibogaine HCl, 98% pure); 1g IV MgSO₄ pre-dosing

papers.title: Ibogaine microdosing in a patient with bipolar depression: case report
papers.key_findings: Microdosing (3mg/72h, 12 weeks) in treatment-resistant bipolar II: 60% HAM-D improvement, no hypomania, stable cardiovascular. Single case, Brazilian regulatory context.

papers.title: Case report: Significant lesion reduction and neural structural changes following ibogaine treatments for multiple sclerosis
papers.key_findings: Two MS patients showed lesion shrinkage and decreased ADC values on MRI after ibogaine, suggesting remyelination. Cortical/subcortical changes in pain/emotion regions. First neuroimaging evidence for ibogaine in MS.

papers.title: A case series of ibogaine toxicity reported to the United Kingdom National Poisons Information Service (NPIS) over a 10-year period
papers.key_findings: 7 UK poison centre cases (2012-2022): 6/7 developed cardiotoxicity; 2 cardiac arrests with hypoxic brain injury; QTc 680-720 msec; no dose-response relationship observed; doses 5-34g root bark.
contraindications: CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (flagged as variable susceptibility factor, not assessed in any case)
papers.dosing_range: Acute: 5–34 g root bark (where reported); chronic: 0.5–0.7 g root bark 5/7 days for 2 months (Patient 6); mixed preparations (root bark, ibogaine HCl tablets, capsules); dose unknown in majority of cases
aliases: UK NPIS Ibogaine Toxicity Case Series

papers.title: Dimethyltryptamine (DMT) and ibogaine elicit membrane effects in HEK cells transiently transfected with the human 5-HT2A receptor
papers.key_findings: Ibogaine (30-100μM) activates membrane currents in 5-HT2AR-transfected HEK cells via NSCC. I197V SNP shows shorter DMT response duration. First demonstration of 5-HT2AR-mediated ionic currents in this model.

papers.title: The psychoactive compound ibogaine sex-dependently alters the firing rate and afterhyperpolarization of Ih-negative neurons in the mouse ventral tegmental area
papers.key_findings: Sex-dependent effects: ibogaine (100μM) decreased AP firing in male VTA GABAergic neurons but not females; altered female AHP kinetics; no membrane current changes; intracellular Ca²⁺ rises in both sexes

papers.title: A Qualitative Inquiry into Practitioners' Experiences of Using Ibogaine and Other Psychoplastogens in the Treatment of Substance Misuse
papers.key_findings: Qualitative study of ibogaine practitioners. Identifies varied dosing strategies, importance of pre/post-treatment integration, holistic patient-centric approaches. Advocates biopsychosocial framework and adaptive regulatory guidelines.

papers.title: Ibogaine for the treatment of Parkinson's disease: A case report
papers.key_findings: First validated-instrument case study of ibogaine for Parkinson's; 80-day low-dose treatment (25-75mg/day) showed improvements in UPDRS, PDQ-39, fatigue, depression; patient reported reduced freezing episodes and enhanced mobility

papers.title: The Ibogaine Experience Scale (IES): Development and psychometric properties of a multidimensional measure of ibogaine's subjective effects
papers.key_findings: PLoS ONE: 70-item Ibogaine Experience Scale (IES) development. 8 dimensions capturing oneiric/dream-like phenomenology. First validated ibogaine-specific psychometric instrument.

papers.title: Is ibogaine treatment durable? 12-month follow-up of magnesium-ibogaine therapy (MISTIC) in Special Operations Veterans with traumatic brain injuries
papers.key_findings: 12-month follow-up of MISTIC trial (n=25). Sustained large effect sizes (d>0.8) for disability, PTSD, depression, anxiety. 84% PTSD remission at 12 months. Dedicated to Nolan Williams (posthumous).
papers.publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC

papers.title: Ibogaine as a Tool in the Process of Overcoming Drug Addiction
papers.key_findings: Brazilian clinical experience confirms ibogaine reduces opioid withdrawal, craving, and substance use; emphasises integration psychotherapy and proper screening as essential; estimates >30,000 treatments since 2007.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions / widened baseline QT interval
Recent cocaine use (QT prolongation for 7-15 days post-cessation)
Low magnesium (correctable — formal but not definitive)

papers.title: Development of Bioisosteric Iboga-alkaloids as Antinociceptive and Anxiolytic Agents with Neuroprotective Effects
papers.key_findings: Benzofuran iboga analogues (C2, C4) show antinociceptive/anxiolytic effects with improved cardiac safety. C4 showed no QTc prolongation in rat ECG. Bioisosteric replacement of indole reduces hERG blockade. BDNF/GABA/dopamine elevation.

papers.title: Harnessing Pharmacogenomics in Clinical Research on Psychedelic-Assisted Therapy
papers.key_findings: CYP2D6 PMs show higher ibogaine exposure and lower noribogaine; halving dose recommended for PMs. CYP2D6 genotyping advised for ibogaine clinical trials.
contraindications: CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (dose reduction to ~50% recommended for ibogaine)
CYP2D6 ultrarapid metaboliser status (may require higher dose or may not be suitable candidates)
Concurrent use of CYP2D6 inhibitors (e.g., paroxetine — causes phenoconversion to PM-like state)
Concurrent use of CYP3A4 inhibitors (e.g., grapefruit juice — potent intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition)
Liver transplant recipients (donor liver genotype controls recipient CYP2D6 phenotype)
Comorbidities affecting hepatic function (cancer, liver disease — can cause phenoconversion)
papers.dosing_range: Standard ibogaine dosing discussed in context of CYP2D6-dependent metabolism; PMs recommended ~50% dose reduction; 20 mg single dose used in referenced Glue et al. 2015 PK study

papers.title: Deciphering Ibogaine's Matrix Pharmacology: Multiple Transporter Modulation at Serotonin Synapses
papers.key_findings: Defines 'matrix pharmacology' - ibogaine/noribogaine modulate VMAT2 (sub-µM), SERT, OCT2 simultaneously. Dual VMAT2+SERT inhibition explains neurochemical effects. Not substrate-type releasers. No catalepsy unlike other VMAT2 inhibitors.
papers.journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society

papers.title: Efficient and modular synthesis of ibogaine and related alkaloids
papers.key_findings: Gram-scale 7-step ibogaine synthesis from pyridine (Olson lab). Enables analogue production. Unnatural (+)-ibogaine lacks psychoplastogenic effects. (-)-10-fluoroibogamine shows exceptional neuroplasticity and SERT modulation.

papers.title: A Thematic Analysis of the Subjective Effects and Phenomenology of Ibogaine Administered in a Clinical Setting
papers.key_findings: Largest phenomenology study (n=471). Themes: emotional amplification, life review, perceptual alteration, visionary states, ibogaine's perceived agency. Beond clinical setting. Open-ended questions capture experiential depth.

papers.title: New Approaches to Old Targets: Re-envisioning the Synthesis of Ibogaine and Immunogenic Glycolipids
papers.key_findings: MSc thesis developing novel chiral and racemic synthetic routes to ibogaine via asymmetric Diels-Alder and Robinson annulation strategies; reviews all prior total syntheses

papers.title: Ibogaine: Revisiting an Ancient Alkaloid for Modern Opioid Dependence
papers.key_findings: Short commentary from Texas pharmacists advocating ibogaine research for opioid dependence; reviews mechanisms and safety; notes Texas 2025 legislation funding FDA ibogaine trials

papers.title: Ibogaine's potential role in supporting reward system recovery across diagnostic boundaries
papers.key_findings: Transdiagnostic hypothesis: ibogaine restores reward system via GDNF, neuroplasticity, and DMN modulation. Proposes unified mechanism across OUD, depression, PTSD, TBI.

papers.title: Treating War Trauma with Ibogaine and 5-MeO-DMT
papers.key_findings: Mission Within protocol: Ibo+5-MeO-DMT sequence for veterans, 850+ treated 2017-2023, pre-treatment EKG/electrolytes, 3-phase integration model
contraindications: Prolonged QT interval (specific EKG exclusion criterion)
Opioids, alcohol, or stimulants in participant's system (urine drug screen required on arrival)
Certain supplements (e.g. milk thistle — CYP2D6 inhibitor)

papers.title: A Study of the Inhibitory and Pharmacological Chaperone Effects of Bupropion and Ibogaine on Monoamine Transporters
papers.key_findings: PhD thesis (preview only) characterising ibogaine/bupropion binding to DAT and SERT; distinct allosteric site differences between transporters; AlphaFold3 predicts peripheral S2 site for ibogaine on DAT.

papers.title: Tolerabilidade e eficácia da ibogaína no tratamento do transtorno por uso de álcool: um estudo aberto e de escalonamento de doses
papers.key_findings: First worldwide clinical trial of ibogaine for AUD. Dose-escalation 20–400mg HCl in N=9. Dose-dependent QTc prolongation (peaks ~496ms). 2/9 abstinent at 3 months.
papers.publisher: Universidade de São Paulo. Agência de Bibliotecas e Coleções Digitais

papers.title: Studies on the iboga cults: IV. The ethnobotanical complex
papers.key_findings: Defines 'ethnobotanical complex' of Bwiti: iboga as primary source with kola, cannabis, tobacco, alan, palm wine additives. Reveals multi-plant ritual system around ancestor worship.

papers.title: Totalna Sinteza Ibogaina (Total Synthesis of Ibogaine)
papers.key_findings: Croatian literature review covering six total synthesis approaches (1966-2025); documents evolution from Büchi's 15-step 0.2% yield to Iyer's 7-step 14% gram-scale synthesis

papers.title: Risk management in a global market of alternative addiction care: a case study of the ibogaine medical subculture
papers.key_findings: 7-year ethnographic study of ibogaine subculture risk management. Documents bottom-up governance without state regulation. Analyzes medicalization, market-based healthcare, knowledge production by drug users and harm reduction activists.

papers.title: Exploring Ibogaine/5-MeO-DMT as Treatment for the Relief of PTSD Symptoms in Veterans
papers.key_findings: N=12 special ops veterans, 10-36 months post-treatment. 100% reported immediate PTSD relief. Grounded theory identifies 'gnosis' as healing mechanism—direct experiential knowledge via transpersonal encounter enables sustained transformation.

papers.title: Not Losing Momentum: Cross-Sectional Insights into Ibogaine Clinical Trials
papers.key_findings: Cross-sectional analysis of 9 ibogaine/noribogaine clinical trials from global registries. Documents methodological heterogeneity, early-phase dominance, and variable cardiac safety protocols. Calls for standardised framework.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiovascular disease or prolonged QT interval (exclusion criterion across multiple trials)
Major psychiatric disorders excluding target SUD (common exclusion)
Recent illicit drug use (variable washout periods across trials)
Concurrent methadone use without stable dosing (trial-specific)
Abnormal baseline ECG findings (QTcF >450 ms in NCT06480981)
papers.dosing_range: Ibogaine: 20–600 mg fixed or 3–12 mg/kg weight-based; Noribogaine: 3–180 mg (across 9 trials)

papers.title: The Use of Ibogaine in the Treatment of Substance Use Disorders: Integrating Psychoactive Experiences as Part of the Recovery Journey
papers.key_findings: PhD thesis: ibogaine phenomenology and narrative self-transformation. Participants report ongoing journey not discrete event. Challenges fixed outcome measurement; supports processual frameworks.

papers.title: Magnesium–ibogaine therapy effects on cortical oscillations and neural complexity in veterans with traumatic brain injury
papers.key_findings: EEG shows theta-alpha power increase (d=0.45–0.61), beta-gamma decrease (d=0.50–0.73), reduced neural complexity and PAF (d=1.05) post-MISTIC; PAF and LZc reductions persist at 1 month; theta/beta ratio correlates with cognitive inhibition gains.
contraindications: Concurrent psychiatric medications (required discontinuation prior to treatment)
Concurrent benzodiazepine use (required discontinuation prior to treatment)
papers.dosing_range: 12.1 ± 1.2 mg/kg oral (ibogaine HCl) + IV magnesium sulfate

papers.title: Reconsidering Ibogaine for the treatment of severe mental illness and substance use disorders
papers.key_findings: Editorial calling for ibogaine RCTs with CYP2D6 pharmacogenomics, safer analogues (18-MC, TBG), and inpatient cardiac monitoring. Reviews Texas $50M initiative and state-level policy shifts.
papers.journal: Journal of Medicine, Surgery, and Public Health

papers.title: Rare but relevant: Ibogaine and cardiovascular complications—prolonged QT interval and ventricular arrhythmias
papers.key_findings: QTc prolongation occurs at therapeutic doses even without pre-existing cardiac conditions; CYP2D6 poor metabolisers at higher risk; safe dose ~0.87 mg/kg (10× lower than treatment doses); recommends genotyping + monitoring.
contraindications: Doses exceeding ~0.87 mg/kg without cardiac monitoring

papers.title: Neurorestorative Properties of Ibogaine: Linking Multi-Receptor Affinities to Remyelination and Metabolic Restoration
papers.key_findings: Proposes ibogaine's multi-receptor affinities (kappa opioid, NMDA, sigma-2) facilitate remyelination and metabolic restoration, providing mechanistic rationale for treating OUD, MS, and TBI via white matter repair

papers.title: Ibogaine: Therapeutic Potential, Cardiac Safety, and Translational Perspectives in the Treatment of Substance Use Disorders—A Scoping Review
papers.key_findings: Most comprehensive cardiac safety review to date; synthesises QTc prolongation, arrhythmias, 19+ fatalities; mechanistic analysis of hERG blockade and noribogaine-driven delayed cardiotoxicity

papers.title: Increased cortical thickness and decreased brain age among special operations veterans with blast TBI after a magnesium-ibogaine protocol
papers.key_findings: MISTIC therapy associated with increased cortical thickness in 13 regions, subcortical expansion in 8 regions, and predicted brain age reduction of 1.3 years at 1 month in SOF veterans with blast TBI (n=25)
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease (QT prolongation risk with ibogaine)
Pre-existing medical conditions associated with ibogaine fatalities
papers.dosing_range: 12.05 mg/kg (SD 1.44) oral ibogaine HCl with IV magnesium sulfate (1 mg pre- and post-ibogaine)

papers.title: Indole Alkaloids as Biased Opioid Receptor Modulators
papers.key_findings: Indole alkaloids (ibogaine, noribogaine, mitragynine) are biased μ/κ-opioid agonists activating G-protein without β-arrestin recruitment, potentially avoiding respiratory depression and tolerance.

papers.title: Psilocybin and Ibogaine as Therapeutic Candidates for Cocaine Addiction: Insights from a Rat Self-Administration Model
papers.key_findings: Rat IVSA: ibogaine (10+40mg/kg) and psilocybin reduced cocaine-seeking and facilitated extinction. Novel two-dose protocol. No reinstatement effects.

papers.title: Accelerated recovery using magnesium ibogaine: characterizing the subjective experience of its rapid healing from neuropsychiatric disorders
papers.key_findings: MISTIC veterans (n=30) report four phenomenological domains: dialogic trauma reappraisal, altered-self/mystical connection, emotional resolution, and embodied brain healing—framed as accelerated auto-psychotherapy.

papers.title: Ibogaine for Opioid Use Disorder: An Unrecognized Risk
papers.key_findings: Commentary warning that ibogaine promotion opposing MOUD may increase overdose risk; 41% of OUD-diagnosed survey respondents had tried ibogaine; includes fatal case report

papers.title: 2014 Ibogaine Client Information Sheet
papers.key_findings: NZ client preparation document: informed consent, treatment expectations, safety protocols for ibogaine therapy.
contraindications: Undisclosed medical history (full disclosure required for safety screening)
Concurrent substance use during treatment (document warns this could be fatal)
Reduced post-treatment opioid tolerance (risk of fatal overdose if substances taken during or after therapy)

papers.title: Clinical Guidelines for Ibogaine-Assisted Detoxification
papers.key_findings: GOLD STANDARD: Comprehensive ibogaine clinical guidelines. ISO31001 risk framework, absolute contraindications, electrolyte targets (K 4.5-5.5, Mg 1.5-2.5), medication clearance timelines.
contraindications: QTc >450ms (M) or >470ms (F); borderline 430-470ms requires cardiologist review; >500ms requires immediate hospital transfer
Epilepsy (rule out benzodiazepine/alcohol withdrawal seizures misdiagnosed as epilepsy)
Major respiratory conditions (emphysema, COPD, cystic fibrosis)
Severe/chronic GI issues (bleeding ulcer, leaky gut syndrome)
Abnormal electrolytes — must correct to preferred ranges: K 4.5-5.5 mEq/L, Mg 1.5-2.5 mEq/L
Liver enzymes >2.5x normal; impaired kidney function; abnormal BUN/creatinine
Within 6 months of major surgery (require physician clearance)
QT-prolonging medications, foods and supplements (must be cleared)
CYP2D6-metabolised drugs (competition for ibogaine metabolic pathway)
Serotonin-increasing medications — SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs, buspirone, trazodone, tramadol, St John's Wort (serotonin syndrome risk)
Centrally acting drugs — blood pressure medications, barbiturates, muscle relaxants, antipsychotics, anticonvulsants, general anaesthetics (interactions not well understood)
Benzodiazepine or alcohol withdrawal (seizure risk — must be stabilised, never ceased acutely; factor in multiple fatalities)
Stimulant use: cocaine ≥7 days clearance; methamphetamine ≥5 days; prescription stimulants ≥5 days; caffeine ≥5 days
Antipsychotic medications — haloperidol/chlorpromazine cause prolonged negative reactions with ibogaine; must not be used as intervention
Corticosteroids (prolong QT interval; caution if required as intervention)
Calcium channel blockers or beta blockers (lower BP/HR dramatically, alter cardiac conduction — beta blockers must be tapered over 7-14 days, not stopped acutely)

papers.title: Manual for Ibogaine Therapy: Screening, Safety, Monitoring & Aftercare
papers.key_findings: Foundational treatment manual establishing 15-20 mg/kg dosing, 48-hour monitoring, EKG screening, and inclusion/exclusion criteria that shaped all subsequent ibogaine protocols
contraindications: Significant cardiac artery blockage or history of myocardial infarction
Blood pressure >170/105 or <80/60; pulse >120 or <50 bpm
Concurrent drug use during treatment (potentiates opiate/stimulant toxicity)
Active neurological or psychiatric disorders requiring treatment
Beck Depression Inventory score ≥24 (debated by contributing authors)
Concomitant medications causing adverse interactions (anti-epileptics, antidepressants, neuroleptics)
History of sensitivity or adverse reaction to ibogaine
GI, liver, or kidney disease compromising metabolism/excretion
Active peptic ulcer (nonfatal adverse events documented)
papers.dosing_range: 15–20 mg/kg therapeutic; 2 mg/kg test dose; 25–300 mg/day low-dose

papers.title: Ibogaine: Information and Guidelines for Integrated Therapy
papers.key_findings: Comprehensive NZ operational manual: inclusion criteria, pre-acceptance screening, medication contraindications, emergency response protocols, consent forms, risk management for regulated therapy.
contraindications: Cardiac issues (pericarditis, history of cardiac problems, elevated cholesterol)
Compromised liver function (active chronic HepC >4x normal range)
Active neurological/psychiatric disorders (psychosis, bipolar, schizophrenia, organic brain disease, dementia requiring ongoing treatment)
Pregnancy/breastfeeding (unless weaning plan incorporated prior to treatment)
CYP2D6-metabolised drugs (inhibitors in particular; caution with inducers and substrates)
Steroids, hormones, depo-injection drugs (caution; corticosteroids may induce psychological disturbances)
Methadone >50mg (must reduce to <50mg; switch to short-acting opioid preferred)
Suboxone/subutex (must switch to short-acting opioid minimum 6 weeks prior)
papers.dosing_range: 10–22 mg/kg (treatment range); 200mg test dose; minimum 72h monitoring

papers.title: Notes for Ibogaine Treatment Providers
papers.key_findings: Pre-GITA UK provider protocol (2001-02): 15-20 mg/kg oral HCl for addiction, 10-12 mg/kg psychospiritual; ECG + LFT screening; gender-differentiated responses observed across 18 treatments
contraindications: Any ECG abnormalities or previous heart problems
Long-term medication with no ibogaine interaction data
Severe asthma combined with heavy smoking drug use
Active stomach ulcer (risk of oesophageal bleeding from retching)
papers.dosing_range: 15-20 mg/kg oral HCl (addiction interruption); 10-12 mg/kg oral HCl (psychospiritual); 17-19 mg/kg most effective range

papers.title: Uncovering Ibogaine: The Deborah Mash Interview
papers.key_findings: First-person account of FDA Phase I trial launch, noribogaine discovery, coca-ethylene backstory, neurotoxicity debate rebuttal, and funding barriers to ibogaine clinical development
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease (woman with 'very sick heart' cited as contraindication violation in Baumann death)
Unknown source material (purity/identity of ibogaine unverifiable outside clinical supply)
papers.dosing_range: 2 mg/kg increments in dose-escalation design up to 14–15 mg/kg target; Mash states underground doses 'way too high' and could be cut by half

papers.title: Clare Wilkins at Horizons 2010: Over 400 Ibogaine Sessions
papers.key_findings: 92% of clients leave detoxed; 18% maintain abstinence 6mo-4yrs; outcome is engagement not abstinence; family systems approach essential; MAPS study protocol launched

papers.title: Clare Wilkins at MAPS 2010: Evolution of Ibogaine Treatment
papers.key_findings: Peer-driven treatment model developed; importance of staff who have taken ibogaine; stabilisation before dosing; deaths analysed to improve safety protocols; integration of meditation, nutrition, bodywork

papers.title: Clare Wilkins at MAPS 2014: Architecture of Emergency Using Psychedelics During the Anthropocene Era
papers.key_findings: Evolved from 'flood' to incremental dosing; total alkaloid safer than HCl; family-centred treatment model; integration crucial for sustained outcomes; collaboration with MAPS leading to clinical trials

papers.title: Clare Wilkins at Breaking Convention 2017: Novel Approach to Methadone Detox
papers.key_findings: ICEERS pilot study: 6-week cumulative dosing protocol for 17-year methadone patient; cannabis oil as adjunct; QTc stayed within safe range; 20 months methadone-free at time of talk
papers.dosing_range: Cumulative low-dose over 6 weeks: 1.5mg/kg → 3mg/kg → 5mg/kg → 10mg/kg (600mg peak dose)

papers.title: Clare Wilkins at EntheoScience 2017: Iboga, Cannabis, Ethics and Alliance
papers.key_findings: Cannabis oil as adjunct to ibogaine for sleep and pain; 40% cartel profit reduction from cannabis legalisation linked to fentanyl crisis; GITA alliance essential for movement unity; rat park communities forming around treatment centres

papers.title: Clare Wilkins at Psychedelic Science 2017: Novel Approach to Methadone Detox
papers.key_findings: ICEERS cumulative dosing protocol; ibogaine as adaptogen at lower doses; QTc monitoring throughout; 20 months methadone-free outcome; extensive Q&A with Paul Glue and audience
papers.dosing_range: Cumulative low-dose over 6 weeks: 1mg/kg → 3mg/kg → 5mg/kg; peak 500mg dose; highest QTc 444ms at hour 3

papers.title: Clare Wilkins on MYEBOGA Podcast (2020): 800+ Treatments and Community Reflections
papers.key_findings: 800+ treatments facilitated; cumulative dosing protocol development after fatality analysis; orthomolecular medicine integration; GITA restructuring; Parkinson's treatment case studies emerging; community fragmentation and need for unity

papers.title: Clare Wilkins at Microdose 2021: A Holotropic Perspective on Networks, Fissures, and Reciprocity
papers.key_findings: Lotsoff's advice: 'Watch QT intervals'; community trauma and division post-Howard's death; three 15-year life phases—trauma, using, ibogaine work

papers.title: Reflections on an Ibogaine Experience
papers.key_findings: First-person 1989 Amsterdam ibogaine treatment account documenting childhood memory revisitation, rebirthing visions, African-themed imagery, withdrawal resolution, and the founding of INTASH self-help movement

papers.title: Professor Jan Bastiaans and the Ibogaine Saga
papers.key_findings: Firsthand account of ibogaine's formative years (1988–1993) in the Netherlands; documents Bastiaans as first psychiatrist to recognise ibogaine's trauma-healing potential underlying addiction recovery

papers.title: Ibogaine & Eboga Experiences: First-Person Accounts from the MyEboga Community
papers.key_findings: 12 first-person ibogaine experience reports (6 chemical dependence, 6 non-chemical dependence) documenting visionary phases, childhood memory recall, fear processing, and sustained post-treatment shifts

papers.title: Expert Testimony: Kentucky Opioid Abatement Advisory Commission Public Hearing
papers.key_findings: Medical ethnography validates lay ibogaine opioid detox. 19 autopsies show deaths preventable with cardiac monitoring. Ibogaine is NOT an opioid agonist—novel mechanism.
papers.journal: Kentucky Opioid Abatement Advisory Commission Public Hearing

papers.title: He Took a Psychedelic to Cure His Addiction. It Was His Last Trip
papers.key_findings: Rolling Stone investigation: 49-year-old American died at Beond clinic Cancún. Documents industry safety failures, inadequate screening, regulatory gaps in unregulated ibogaine treatment settings.

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 1: The War With the Junkies
papers.key_findings: Documents the cultural war between the counterculture pot movement and heroin infiltration of the NYC underground press/music scene (1978–1980), establishing the political conditions that drove ibogaine development.

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 10: Carlo Contoreggi
papers.key_findings: Contoreggi — first government clinician to witness ibogaine treatments. Dragonheart case: $500/day heroin addict transformed in 2 weeks. Jaffee/Sharpe study debunked by Glick.

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 11: Geerte F.
papers.key_findings: Geerte's two ibogaine treatments (1989, 1991). DASH (Dutch Addict Self-Help) founded. Ten treatments using Bwiti-informed ritual protocol. Relapse analysis: social environment outweighs pharmacology.
contraindications: Resistance/fight-or-flight response can compromise treatment (Geerte's first treatment)
Sound hypersensitivity during treatment requires quiet environment
Social environment of active users post-treatment is primary relapse risk
papers.dosing_range: Weight-adjusted oral ibogaine HCl; allergy dose then full dose

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 12: VALIS
papers.key_findings: VALIS as gnostic framework for ibogaine phenomenology. Cerebellar hypothesis — addiction as deep conditioning. Baker: first HIV+ treatment. Lamontia: first rigorous follow-up. REM-like EEG data.
papers.dosing_range: 35mg methadone detox (Lamontia); 125mg methadone detox (unnamed); 80mg methadone + $250 heroin/day (Baker) — describes addiction burden treated, not ibogaine dose

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 13: Bwiti
papers.key_findings: Bwiti cosmogony as experiential framework for ibogaine visions. VALIS/Gnostic parallels to Fang ancestral memory. Lamontia off 35mg methadone in 3 days. Ibogaine/cocaine efficacy consensus (1992).

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 14: Time Is a Child at Play
papers.key_findings: Cerebellar reset hypothesis drawing on Heath, Harlow, Prescott, chaos theory. One ibogaine-associated fatality (23yo female, Germany 1993). REM-like random activation as addiction interrupt.
contraindications: Concurrent opioid use during or after ibogaine treatment (German case: heroin use 22hrs post-dosing caused fatal respiratory depression)
Inadequate screening and motivation assessment (patient reportedly did not want to quit)
Unsupervised access to drugs during treatment period
aliases: Time Is a Child at Play

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 15: Molliver's Travels
papers.key_findings: 1993 FDA Advisory Panel: Molliver-Mash confrontation over cerebellar toxicity (rats vs primates). Purkinje cell excitotoxicity, vincristine comparison, REM-reset hypothesis. ACT UP organising.
contraindications: Cerebellar Purkinje cell loss at sub-lethal doses in rats (100 mg/kg = 10-15% loss; 25 mg/kg = <1%)
Trunkal ataxia during treatment (vestibular disruption via vermis excitation)
Potential thalamic and locus coeruleus secondary excitotoxicity at high doses
papers.dosing_range: 100 mg/kg sub-lethal in rats (~80% LD50); 25 mg/kg minimal toxicity in rats; 16–18 mg/kg estimated standard human dose; FDA-approved trial doses 1, 2, and 5 mg/kg

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 16: The Passover Plot
papers.key_findings: Gnostic sacrament lineage from Zoroastrian haoma through Mithras to medieval suppression. Molliver primate safety data (no toxicity 150mg/kg oral). Sisko NIDA speech and Baltimore advocacy.
contraindications: Seizures observed with intraperitoneal ibogaine injection in primates (Linda Aronhoff disclosure)
papers.dosing_range: 150 mg/kg oral (no toxicity in primates); 50 mg/kg i.p. required to duplicate ataxia in primates

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 17: Cures Not Wars
papers.key_findings: Documents May 1994 NIDA Phase I/II protocol design session (60-subject cohort, nested dose run-up). NAS report endorsed ACT UP demands. Molliver primate data: no toxicity 150mg/kg oral. Kappa NDE model, endogenous nor-harman discovery.
contraindications: Women excluded from initial Phase I/II trial (greater sensitivity across species)
HIV+ or TB+ excluded from initial protocol
Seizures observed with intraperitoneal ibogaine injection in primates
Extreme caution in aged (rapid healing from sleep restoration could trigger latent antigens)
papers.dosing_range: 150mg, 300mg, 600mg, 900mg, possible 1200mg (nested dose run-up); 150mg/kg oral no toxicity in primates; 50mg/kg i.p. required for ataxia in primates

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 18: Deborah Mash's Brain
papers.key_findings: Detailed insider account of 1994-95 NIDA ibogaine review, Panama treatments, Mash's noribogaine pharmacokinetics, three ibogaine-associated deaths, and political forces that blocked the Phase I/II trial.
contraindications: Pre-existing coronary artery disease (80% arterial sclerosis cited as contraindication violation in Baumann death)
Concurrent opioid use at time of dosing (opiates in urine at time of Baumann treatment)
CYP2D6 slow metaboliser status (prolonged noribogaine exposure implicated in Nicola K. death)
Female sensitivity — lower dosing recommended (split dosing used for Nancy's third treatment)
papers.dosing_range: 8–31 mg/kg oral (range across Panama treatments); FDA approved Miami protocol at 8 mg/kg; Baumann used ~half recommended dose; Nancy received 25, 29–31, and split 10+20 mg/kg across three treatments

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 2: Howard Lotsof
papers.key_findings: Documents Lotsof's 1962–63 ibogaine discovery: 5 of 7 addicts quit heroin/cocaine involuntarily for 6+ months; Isbell's 1955 MK-Ultra sub-therapeutic trials at Lexington; ibogaine's Schedule I placement linked to Rockefeller/methadone politics.
papers.dosing_range: ~1 g oral for 150 lb person (threshold ~6 mg/kg); Isbell used sub-therapeutic 250 mg

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 3: Dhoruba Moore
papers.key_findings: Documents Black Panther anti-heroin activism, COINTELPRO targeting, French Connection heroin politics, and Lincoln Detox acupuncture origins as racial-political context of addiction treatment.

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 4: Dana Beal
papers.key_findings: Dana Beal biography from Kent State to Reagan era: YIPPIE/White Panther merger, smoke-in movement, McGovern confrontation on CIA heroin, ALD-52 vision at Spokane, and the political trajectory from marijuana activism to ibogaine advocacy.

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 5: The Staten Island Project
papers.key_findings: Origins of ibogaine development 1981-1986: Lotsof's literature review, three patent filings (morphine, alcohol, cocaine/amphetamine), NIDA rejections, and Clouet's early mechanistic validation.

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 6: Bob Sisko
papers.key_findings: Sisko's 9 paraclinical treatments (1987-1991) — first new human ibogaine trials in 25 years. Ibogaine interrupts cigarettes and methadone. Multiple case studies with Bwiti phenomenology.
contraindications: Enabling behaviour from untreated partner (methadone smuggling documented)
Pre-existing cardiac conditions (Baumann fatality reference — weak heart)
papers.dosing_range: Weight-adjusted oral ibogaine HCl (99.7% purity); booster doses administered 1.5hr post-initial if needed; rectal aqueous solution as emergency anti-emetic route

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 7: Stanley Glick
papers.key_findings: First dose-response data: 5mg/kg gives 40% morphine reduction, 40mg gives 90%. After-effect persisted weeks. Transient kappa-opioid binding. Long-lasting metabolite hypothesis prefigures noribogaine.
papers.dosing_range: 2.5–80 mg/kg IP ibogaine in rats; 5–40 mg/kg IP dose-response on morphine self-administration; 40–80 mg/kg IP for persistent after-effect

papers.title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 8: Nico Adriaans
papers.key_findings: Nico Adriaans (founder, Dutch Junkiebond) treated May 1990 for 0.75g/day heroin. Three-phase phenomenology documented. Inadvertent cigarette interruption. Methadone 180 to 30mg.
papers.dosing_range: Weight-adjusted oral ibogaine HCl (allergy test dose 10mg, then full dose in two halves 30min apart)

papers.title: Soneira and the Scramble for Ibogaine: Could the Iboga Plant Ever Be Integrated into Mainstream Western Healthcare?
papers.key_findings: Investigates Soneira's $30M fundraise, Stanford patent controversy over ibogaine+magnesium for TBI, and tensions between indigenous IP, practitioner knowledge, and academic commercialisation.

papers.title: Learning from ibogaine fatalities, rather than covering them up
papers.key_findings: Documents 5 ibogaine fatalities (2024-2026): Ambio, SoulCentro, IbogaQuest, Holos/Costa Rica, Haman/Colorado; advocates transparency over cover-ups; cites 1-in-427 fatality estimate; GITA warnings on nitazene/xylazine detection failures
papers.dosing_range: Not specified (flood dose protocols at various clinics)

papers.title: Ibogaine & Eboga Experiences: First-Person Accounts from the MyEboga Community
papers.key_findings: 12 first-person ibogaine experience reports (6 chemical dependence, 6 non-chemical dependence) documenting visionary phases, childhood memory recall, fear processing, and sustained post-treatment shifts

papers.title: Ibogaine Related Fatalities: Compilation of Public Domain Case Reports 1990–2010
papers.key_findings: Compilation of 12 ibogaine/iboga-related fatalities (1990–2010) from public domain sources; pre-existing cardiac disease, polysubstance use, and absent medical screening identified as recurring risk factors
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease (prior MI, congenital defects, vascular disease)
Concurrent methadone use without adequate taper
Concurrent benzodiazepine or opioid use during treatment
Absence of pre-treatment medical screening (ECG, liver function)
History of cardiac surgery or congenital heart defects
papers.dosing_range: 4.5–29 mg/kg oral (HCl); 500mg–6g (extract/root bark); dose unknown in several cases

papers.title: Ibogaine: A Clinical Summary
papers.key_findings: Industry marketing document, contradicts Alper 2012 on deaths, safety claims vs evidence mismatch.
contraindications: Certain cardiovascular conditions (unspecified — refers to clinical guidelines)
Coincident withdrawal from alcohol or benzodiazepines
Substances metabolised by CYP450-2D6 (including grapefruit and quinine)

papers.title: The Future of Iboga: Perspectives from Central Africa — Phase II Report
papers.key_findings: Qualitative field study (n=56 interviews, Gabon 2019) documents Bwiti perspectives on iboga sustainability, spiritual healing mechanisms, cultivation methods, and Nagoya Protocol compliance.

papers.title: Ibogaine
authors: Legislative Analysis and Public Policy Association
papers.key_findings: US legislative analysis: ibogaine Schedule I in 46 states; 7 states introduced access/research bills in 2025; Colorado and DC decriminalisation frameworks surveyed
